1→# Dashboard Widgets Guide
     2→
     3→**Last Updated:** November 18, 2025
     4→
     5→## Overview
     6→
     7→Neuman Brain Dashboard includes **49 widgets** across 4 categories. All widgets are **free forever** (no Pro tier required).
     8→
     9→**Categories:**
    10→- 🎯 Productivity (6 widgets) - Task management, time tracking, quick actions
    11→- 📰 Information (14 widgets) - News, weather, facts, entertainment
    12→- 🛠️ Utility (14 widgets) - Calculators, converters, generators
    13→- 💻 Developer/Tech (15 widgets) - GitHub, package stats, tech news
    14→
    15→---
    16→
    17→## 🎯 Productivity Widgets (6)
    18→
    19→### 1. TaskSummaryWidget
    20→**What it does:** Shows task overview with status breakdown (todo, in-progress, done)
    21→**Use case:** Morning dashboard to see today's priorities
    22→**Configuration:** Filter by status, due date range
    23→**Keyboard shortcuts:** Click card to navigate to Kanban
    24→
    25→### 2. UpcomingEventsWidget
    26→**What it does:** Displays next 7 days of calendar events
    27→**Use case:** Week-ahead planning
    28→**Configuration:** Number of days to show (1-30)
    29→**Features:** Click event to open calendar, shows event time & description
    30→
    31→### 3. RecentNotesWidget
    32→**What it does:** Shows last 5 edited notes
    33→**Use case:** Quick access to active work
    34→**Configuration:** Number of notes (3-10)
    35→**Features:** Click note to open editor, shows last modified time
    36→
    37→### 4. QuickAddWidget
    38→**What it does:** Rapid creation for tasks, events, notes
    39→**Use case:** Capture ideas without navigating away
    40→**Configuration:** Default entity type
    41→**Keyboard shortcuts:** Enter to create, Tab to switch type
    42→
    43→### 5. TasksQuickAddWidget
    44→**What it does:** Fast task creation with priority & due date
    45→**Use case:** Inbox capture workflow
    46→**Configuration:** Default column, auto-assign
    47→**Features:** One-click add to specific Kanban column
    48→
    49→### 6. PomodoroWidget
    50→**What it does:** 25/5 minute Pomodoro timer
    51→**Use case:** Focus sessions with breaks
    52→**Configuration:** Work duration (15-60 min), break duration (5-15 min)
    53→**Features:** Audio notifications, session tracking
    54→
    55→---
    56→
    57→## 📰 Information Widgets (14)
    58→
    59→### 7. WeatherForecastWidget
    60→**What it does:** Current weather + 7-day forecast
    61→**Use case:** Daily planning
    62→**Configuration:** Location (auto-detect or manual), units (°F/°C)
    63→**API:** Open-Meteo (free, no API key required)
    64→
    65→### 8. AINewsWidget
    66→**What it does:** Latest AI/ML news headlines
    67→**Use case:** Stay current with AI developments
    68→**Features:** Click headline to read full article, auto-refresh hourly
    69→
    70→### 9. HackerNewsWidget
    71→**What it does:** Top stories from Hacker News
    72→**Use case:** Tech industry news
    73→**Configuration:** Story count (5-20), sort by (top, new, best)
    74→
    75→### 10. RedditWidget
    76→**What it does:** Posts from configured subreddits
    77→**Use case:** Community discussions
    78→**Configuration:** Subreddit list, post count, sort method
    79→
    80→### 11. DevToWidget
    81→**What it does:** Trending dev.to articles
    82→**Use case:** Developer tutorials and insights
    83→**Features:** Tags filter, click to read on dev.to
    84→
    85→### 12. ProductHuntWidget
    86→**What it does:** Today's top products from Product Hunt
    87→**Use case:** Discover new tools
    88→**Features:** Upvote count, category tags, launch date
    89→
    90→### 13. GitHubTrendingWidget
    91→**What it does:** Trending repositories today/this week
    92→**Use case:** Discover popular projects
    93→**Configuration:** Language filter, time range
    94→**Features:** Stars count, description preview
    95→
    96→### 14. QuoteWidget
    97→**What it does:** Daily inspirational quote
    98→**Use case:** Motivation
    99→**Configuration:** Category (inspirational, funny, tech)
   100→**Features:** Share quote, refresh for new quote
   101→
   102→### 15. MotivationalWidget
   103→**What it does:** Motivational quotes and affirmations
   104→**Use case:** Positive mindset
   105→**Features:** Auto-rotate quotes, custom quote collection
   106→
   107→### 16. WordOfDayWidget
   108→**What it does:** Vocabulary builder with definitions
   109→**Use case:** Language learning
   110→**Features:** Pronunciation, examples, save to favorites
   111→
   112→### 17. FactsWidget
   113→**What it does:** Random interesting facts
   114→**Use case:** Entertainment, conversation starters
   115→**Features:** Refresh for new fact, share fact
   116→
   117→### 18. JokeWidget
   118→**What it does:** Programming jokes and puns
   119→**Use case:** Lighten the mood
   120→**Features:** Refresh for new joke, joke categories
   121→
   122→### 19. SportsWidget
   123→**What it does:** Live scores and upcoming games
   124→**Use case:** Sports fans
   125→**Configuration:** Favorite teams, leagues
   126→
   127→### 20. WikipediaWidget
   128→**What it does:** Featured article of the day
   129→**Use case:** Learn something new
   130→**Features:** Click to read full article on Wikipedia
   131→
   132→---
   133→
   134→## 🛠️ Utility Widgets (14)
   135→
   136→### 21. CalculatorWidget
   137→**What it does:** Basic calculator (add, subtract, multiply, divide)
   138→**Use case:** Quick calculations
   139→**Keyboard shortcuts:** Number keys, operators, Enter to calculate
   140→
   141→### 22. UnitConverterWidget
   142→**What it does:** Convert length, weight, temperature, currency
   143→**Use case:** International collaboration
   144→**Supported units:** 50+ units across 8 categories
   145→**Features:** Real-time conversion, swap direction
   146→
   147→### 23. ColorPaletteWidget
   148→**What it does:** HEX/RGB/HSL color picker
   149→**Use case:** Design work
   150→**Features:** Copy to clipboard, color history, palette generator
   151→
   152→### 24. QRCodeWidget
   153→**What it does:** Generate QR codes from text/URLs
   154→**Use case:** Share links, contact info
   155→**Features:** Download PNG, size adjustment, error correction
   156→
   157→### 25. CurrencyWidget
   158→**What it does:** Live currency conversion
   159→**Use case:** International finance
   160→**Configuration:** Base currency, target currencies
   161→**Features:** Real-time rates, 150+ currencies
   162→
   163→### 26. ClipboardWidget
   164→**What it does:** Clipboard history manager
   165→**Use case:** Copy/paste workflows
   166→**Features:** Search history, pin favorites, clear all
   167→
   168→### 27. CountdownWidget
   169→**What it does:** Count down to important dates
   170→**Use case:** Event planning
   171→**Configuration:** Target date, custom message
   172→**Features:** Days/hours/minutes display, notifications
   173→
   174→### 28. TabManagerWidget
   175→**What it does:** Save and restore browser tab groups
   176→**Use case:** Project context switching
   177→**Features:** Export tab list, restore sessions
   178→
   179→### 29. ShortcutsWidget
   180→**What it does:** Customizable quick links
   181→**Use case:** Bookmark frequently visited sites
   182→**Configuration:** Add/edit/remove links, icons
   183→
   184→### 30. UnsplashWidget
   185→**What it does:** Beautiful daily photos from Unsplash
   186→**Use case:** Visual inspiration
   187→**Features:** Refresh for new photo, photographer credit, download
   188→
   189→### 31. PixelArtWidget
   190→**What it does:** Simple pixel art creator
   191→**Use case:** 8-bit art, icons
   192→**Features:** Grid size (8x8 to 32x32), export PNG
   193→
   194→### 32. MemoryGameWidget
   195→**What it does:** Memory card matching game
   196→**Use case:** Brain training, breaks
   197→**Configuration:** Difficulty (easy/medium/hard)
   198→
   199→### 33. TypingTestWidget
   200→**What it does:** Measure typing speed (WPM)
   201→**Use case:** Skill improvement
   202→**Features:** Accuracy tracking, progress history
   203→
   204→### 34. BoredWidget
   205→**What it does:** Random activity suggestions when bored
   206→**Use case:** Break ideas
   207→**Configuration:** Activity type (education, recreational, social)
   208→
   209→---
   210→
   211→## 💻 Developer/Tech Widgets (15)
   212→
   213→### 35. GitHubWidget
   214→**What it does:** Your GitHub profile stats
   215→**Use case:** Track contributions
   216→**Configuration:** GitHub username
   217→**Features:** Contribution graph, repo count, followers
   218→
   219→### 36. RepoStatsWidget
   220→**What it does:** Specific repository statistics
   221→**Use case:** Monitor project health
   222→**Configuration:** Repository URL
   223→**Features:** Stars, forks, issues, PRs, last commit
   224→
   225→### 37. PackageStatsWidget
   226→**What it does:** npm package download stats
   227→**Use case:** Track package popularity
   228→**Configuration:** Package name
   229→**Features:** Weekly downloads, trend chart, version
   230→
   231→### 38. IPInfoWidget
   232→**What it does:** Your current IP address and location
   233→**Use case:** Network debugging
   234→**Features:** IPv4/IPv6, ISP, location, VPN detection
   235→
   236→### 39. UptimeWidget
   237→**What it does:** Monitor website uptime
   238→**Use case:** Service health tracking
   239→**Configuration:** URL list, check interval
   240→**Features:** Response time, status history
   241→
   242→### 40. CryptoWidget
   243→**What it does:** Cryptocurrency prices
   244→**Use case:** Portfolio tracking
   245→**Configuration:** Coin list (BTC, ETH, etc.)
   246→**Features:** Price change %, 24h chart
   247→
   248→### 41. StockMarketWidget
   249→**What it does:** Stock prices and indices
   250→**Use case:** Market monitoring
   251→**Configuration:** Ticker symbols
   252→**Features:** Real-time prices, day change, pre/post market
   253→
   254→### 42. AnalyticsWidget
   255→**What it does:** Local usage analytics (privacy-first)
   256→**Use case:** Productivity insights
   257→**Features:** Time spent per feature, most-used widgets
   258→
   259→### 43. AirQualityWidget
   260→**What it does:** Air quality index (AQI) for your location
   261→**Use case:** Health awareness
   262→**Configuration:** Location, update interval
   263→**Features:** AQI number, health recommendations
   264→
   265→### 44. DictionaryWidget
   266→**What it does:** Quick word definitions
   267→**Use case:** Writing assistance
   268→**Features:** Synonyms, antonyms, examples, pronunciation
   269→
   270→### 45. TwitchWidget
   271→**What it does:** Live Twitch streams from followed channels
   272→**Use case:** Streamer fans
   273→**Configuration:** Channel list
   274→**Features:** Viewer count, stream title, click to watch
   275→
   276→### 46. YouTubeWidget
   277→**What it does:** Subscribed channels' latest videos
   278→**Use case:** Content discovery
   279→**Configuration:** Channel IDs
   280→**Features:** Thumbnail preview, view count
   281→
   282→### 47. WorldClockWidget
   283→**What it does:** Multiple timezone clocks
   284→**Use case:** Global teams
   285→**Configuration:** Timezone list (up to 6)
   286→**Features:** Analog/digital display, AM/PM/24h
   287→
   288→### 48. AwesomeListsWidget
   289→**What it does:** Curated awesome-* GitHub lists
   290→**Use case:** Resource discovery
   291→**Configuration:** Topic (Python, JavaScript, Machine Learning)
   292→
   293→### 49. WeatherMapWidget
   294→**What it does:** Interactive weather map
   295→**Use case:** Travel planning
   296→**Features:** Radar, temperature layer, zoom/pan
   297→
   298→---
   299→
   300→## Adding Widgets to Your Dashboard
   301→
   302→**Step 1:** Click "Add Widget" button (+ icon) in dashboard header
   303→**Step 2:** Browse or search widget library
   304→**Step 3:** Click widget card to add to dashboard
   305→**Step 4:** Widget appears in next available grid position
   306→**Step 5:** Drag to reposition (if drag-drop enabled)
   307→
   308→## Removing Widgets
   309→
   310→**Method 1:** Hover over widget → Click ⚙️ icon → "Remove"
   311→**Method 2:** Right-click widget → "Remove Widget"
   312→
   313→## Widget Configuration
   314→
   315→Each widget has a settings menu (⚙️ icon):
   316→- **Refresh interval** - How often to update data
   317→- **Display options** - Size, color, theme
   318→- **Data source** - API keys (if required), filters
   319→- **Notifications** - Enable/disable alerts
   320→
   321→## Performance Tips
   322→
   323→- **Limit active widgets:** Keep < 12 widgets for optimal performance
   324→- **Disable auto-refresh:** For widgets you rarely use
   325→- **Lazy loading:** Widgets load only when scrolled into view
   326→- **Bundle impact:** Each widget adds ~1-5KB (lazy loaded)
   327→
   328→---
   329→
   330→## Widget Request
   331→
   332→**Want a widget we don't have?**
   333→- Create GitHub issue: [Feature Request](https://github.com/travisjneuman/system.knowledgebase/issues)
   334→- Include: Widget name, description, use case, data source
   335→
   336→---

# Dashboard Widgets Guide

**Last Updated:** November 18, 2025

## Overview

Neuman Brain Dashboard includes **49 widgets** across 4 categories. All widgets are **free forever** (no Pro tier required).

**Categories:**
- 🎯 Productivity (6 widgets) - Task management, time tracking, quick actions
- 📰 Information (14 widgets) - News, weather, facts, entertainment
- 🛠️ Utility (14 widgets) - Calculators, converters, generators
- 💻 Developer/Tech (15 widgets) - GitHub, package stats, tech news

---

## 🎯 Productivity Widgets (6)

### 1. TaskSummaryWidget
**What it does:** Shows task overview with status breakdown (todo, in-progress, done)
**Use case:** Morning dashboard to see today's priorities
**Configuration:** Filter by status, due date range
**Keyboard shortcuts:** Click card to navigate to Kanban

### 2. UpcomingEventsWidget
**What it does:** Displays next 7 days of calendar events
**Use case:** Week-ahead planning
**Configuration:** Number of days to show (1-30)
**Features:** Click event to open calendar, shows event time & description

### 3. RecentNotesWidget
**What it does:** Shows last 5 edited notes
**Use case:** Quick access to active work
**Configuration:** Number of notes (3-10)
**Features:** Click note to open editor, shows last modified time

### 4. QuickAddWidget
**What it does:** Rapid creation for tasks, events, notes
**Use case:** Capture ideas without navigating away
**Configuration:** Default entity type
**Keyboard shortcuts:** Enter to create, Tab to switch type

### 5. TasksQuickAddWidget
**What it does:** Fast task creation with priority & due date
**Use case:** Inbox capture workflow
**Configuration:** Default column, auto-assign
**Features:** One-click add to specific Kanban column

### 6. PomodoroWidget
**What it does:** 25/5 minute Pomodoro timer
**Use case:** Focus sessions with breaks
**Configuration:** Work duration (15-60 min), break duration (5-15 min)
**Features:** Audio notifications, session tracking

---

## 📰 Information Widgets (14)

### 7. WeatherForecastWidget
**What it does:** Current weather + 7-day forecast

### 8. AINewsWidget
**What it does:** Latest AI/ML news headlines

**What it does:** Top stories from Hacker News
**Use case:** Tech industry news
**Configuration:** Story count (5-20), sort by (top, new, best)

### 10. RedditWidget
**What it does:** Posts from configured subreddits
**Use case:** Community discussions
**Configuration:** Subreddit list, post count, sort method

### 11. DevToWidget
**What it does:** Trending dev.to articles
**Use case:** Developer tutorials and insights
**Features:** Tags filter, click to read on dev.to

### 12. ProductHuntWidget
**What it does:** Today's top products from Product Hunt
**Use case:** Discover new tools
**Features:** Upvote count, category tags, launch date

### 13. GitHubTrendingWidget
**What it does:** Trending repositories today/this week
**Use case:** Discover popular projects
**Configuration:** Language filter, time range
**Features:** Stars count, description preview

### 14. QuoteWidget
**What it does:** Daily inspirational quote
**Use case:** Motivation
**Configuration:** Category (inspirational, funny, tech)
**Features:** Share quote, refresh for new quote

### 15. MotivationalWidget
**What it does:** Motivational quotes and affirmations
**Use case:** Positive mindset
**Features:** Auto-rotate quotes, custom quote collection

### 16. WordOfDayWidget
**What it does:** Vocabulary builder with definitions
**Use case:** Language learning
**Features:** Pronunciation, examples, save to favorites

### 17. FactsWidget
**What it does:** Random interesting facts
**Use case:** Entertainment, conversation starters
**Features:** Refresh for new fact, share fact

### 18. JokeWidget
**What it does:** Programming jokes and puns
**Use case:** Lighten the mood
**Features:** Refresh for new joke, joke categories

### 19. SportsWidget
**What it does:** Live scores and upcoming games
**Use case:** Sports fans

### 20. WikipediaWidget
**What it does:** Featured article of the day
**Use case:** Learn something new

---

## 🛠️ Utility Widgets (14)

### 21. CalculatorWidget
**What it does:** Basic calculator (add, subtract, multiply, divide)
**Use case:** Quick calculations
**Keyboard shortcuts:** Number keys, operators, Enter to calculate

### 22. UnitConverterWidget
**What it does:** Convert length, weight, temperature, currency
**Use case:** International collaboration
**Supported units:** 50+ units across 8 categories
**Features:** Real-time conversion, swap direction

### 23. ColorPaletteWidget
**What it does:** HEX/RGB/HSL color picker
**Use case:** Design work

### 24. QRCodeWidget

**Use case:** Share links, contact info

### 25. CurrencyWidget
**What it does:** Live currency conversion
**Use case:** International finance
**Configuration:** Base currency, target currencies
**Features:** Real-time rates, 150+ currencies

### 26. ClipboardWidget
**What it does:** Clipboard history manager

### 27. CountdownWidget
**What it does:** Count down to important dates
**Use case:** Event planning
**Configuration:** Target date, custom message

### 28. TabManagerWidget
**What it does:** Save and restore browser tab groups
**Use case:** Project context switching
**Features:** Export tab list, restore sessions

### 29. ShortcutsWidget
**What it does:** Customizable quick links
**Use case:** Bookmark frequently visited sites
**Configuration:** Add/edit/remove links, icons

### 30. UnsplashWidget
**What it does:** Beautiful daily photos from Unsplash
**Use case:** Visual inspiration
**Features:** Refresh for new photo, photographer credit, download

**What it does:** Simple pixel art creator
**Use case:** 8-bit art, icons
**Features:** Grid size (8x8 to 32x32), export PNG

**What it does:** Measure typing speed (WPM)

**What it does:** Random activity suggestions when bored

---

## 💻 Developer/Tech Widgets (15)

### 35. GitHubWidget
**What it does:** Your GitHub profile stats
**Use case:** Track contributions
**Configuration:** GitHub username
**Features:** Contribution graph, repo count, followers

### 36. RepoStatsWidget
**What it does:** Specific repository statistics
**Use case:** Monitor project health
**Configuration:** Repository URL
**Features:** Stars, forks, issues, PRs, last commit

### 37. PackageStatsWidget
**What it does:** npm package download stats
**Use case:** Track package popularity
**Configuration:** Package name
**Features:** Weekly downloads, trend chart, version

### 38. IPInfoWidget
**What it does:** Your current IP address and location
**Use case:** Network debugging
**Features:** IPv4/IPv6, ISP, location, VPN detection

### 39. UptimeWidget
**What it does:** Monitor website uptime
**Use case:** Service health tracking
**Configuration:** URL list, check interval
**Features:** Response time, status history

### 40. CryptoWidget
**What it does:** Cryptocurrency prices
**Use case:** Portfolio tracking
**Configuration:** Coin list (BTC, ETH, etc.)
**Features:** Price change %, 24h chart

### 41. StockMarketWidget
**What it does:** Stock prices and indices
**Use case:** Market monitoring
**Configuration:** Ticker symbols
**Features:** Real-time prices, day change, pre/post market

### 42. AnalyticsWidget
**What it does:** Local usage analytics (privacy-first)
**Use case:** Productivity insights
**Features:** Time spent per feature, most-used widgets

### 43. AirQualityWidget
**What it does:** Air quality index (AQI) for your location
**Use case:** Health awareness
**Configuration:** Location, update interval
**Features:** AQI number, health recommendations

### 44. DictionaryWidget
**What it does:** Quick word definitions
**Use case:** Writing assistance
**Features:** Synonyms, antonyms, examples, pronunciation

### 45. TwitchWidget
**What it does:** Live Twitch streams from followed channels
**Use case:** Streamer fans
**Configuration:** Channel list
**Features:** Viewer count, stream title, click to watch

### 46. YouTubeWidget
**What it does:** Subscribed channels' latest videos
**Use case:** Content discovery
**Configuration:** Channel IDs
**Features:** Thumbnail preview, view count

### 47. WorldClockWidget
**What it does:** Multiple timezone clocks
**Use case:** Global teams
**Configuration:** Timezone list (up to 6)
**Features:** Analog/digital display, AM/PM/24h

### 48. AwesomeListsWidget
**What it does:** Curated awesome-* GitHub lists
**Use case:** Resource discovery
**Configuration:** Topic (Python, JavaScript, Machine Learning)

### 49. WeatherMapWidget
**What it does:** Interactive weather map
**Use case:** Travel planning
**Features:** Radar, temperature layer, zoom/pan

---

## Adding Widgets to Your Dashboard

**Step 1:** Click "Add Widget" button (+ icon) in dashboard header
**Step 2:** Browse or search widget library
**Step 3:** Click widget card to add to dashboard
**Step 4:** Widget appears in next available grid position
**Step 5:** Drag to reposition (if drag-drop enabled)

## Removing Widgets

**Method 1:** Hover over widget → Click ⚙️ icon → "Remove"
**Method 2:** Right-click widget → "Remove Widget"

## Widget Configuration

Each widget has a settings menu (⚙️ icon):
- **Refresh interval** - How often to update data
- **Display options** - Size, color, theme
- **Data source** - API keys (if required), filters
- **Notifications** - Enable/disable alerts

## Performance Tips

- **Limit active widgets:** Keep < 12 widgets for optimal performance
- **Disable auto-refresh:** For widgets you rarely use
- **Lazy loading:** Widgets load only when scrolled into view
- **Bundle impact:** Each widget adds ~1-5KB (lazy loaded)

---

## Widget Request

**Want a widget we don't have?**
- Create GitHub issue: [Feature Request](https://github.com/travisjneuman/system.knowledgebase/issues)
- Include: Widget name, description, use case, data source

---

**Last Updated:** November 18, 2025
**Widget Count:** 49 (as of Nov 2025)
**All widgets:** Free forever, no Pro tier required

1→# Storage Migration Guide
     2→
     3→**Last Updated:** November 13, 2025
     4→**Audience:** Neuman Brain users
     5→**Purpose:** Understand storage upgrades and data migration
     6→
     7→---
     8→
     9→## What Changed?
    10→
    11→Neuman Brain has upgraded from **localStorage** (5-10MB limit) to **IndexedDB** (50GB+ capacity) to support:
    12→
    13→✅ **Rich text notes with images** (coming in Q1 2026)
    14→✅ **Large datasets** (thousands of tasks, events, and notes)
    15→✅ **Better performance** (async operations don't block the UI)
    16→✅ **Offline support** (works without internet)
    17→✅ **Future-proof** (room for growth as you add more data)
    18→
    19→---
    20→
    21→## Do I Need to Do Anything?
    22→
    23→**No! Migration happens automatically** when you:
    24→
    25→1. Open Neuman Brain in your browser
    26→2. Use any feature (tasks, calendar, habits, etc.)
    27→3. Data is automatically copied from localStorage to IndexedDB
    28→
    29→You don't need to lift a finger - it just works! 🎉
    30→
    31→---
    32→
    33→## Manual Migration (Optional)
    34→
    35→If you want to migrate everything at once or check migration status:
    36→
    37→### Step-by-Step Instructions
    38→
    39→1. Open **Neuman Brain** in your browser
    40→2. Click **Settings** (gear icon in navigation)
    41→3. Go to **⚙️ Advanced** tab
    42→4. Scroll to **💾 Storage Information** section
    43→5. You'll see one of three states:
    44→
    45→#### State 1: Migration Needed ⚠️
    46→If you see "Migration Status: ⚠️ Needed":
    47→- Click **🚀 Migrate to IndexedDB Now** button
    48→- Wait 5-10 seconds (depends on data size)
    49→- You'll see: "✅ Migration successful! Migrated X items"
    50→
    51→#### State 2: Migration Complete ✅
    52→If you see "Migration Status: ✅ Complete":
    53→- You're all set! Nothing to do.
    54→- Your data is already in IndexedDB.
    55→
    56→#### State 3: Not Needed ✅
    57→If you see "Migration Status: ✅ Not Needed":
    58→- This is a fresh install or you've never used localStorage
    59→- All new data automatically goes to IndexedDB
    60→
    61→---
    62→
    63→## What Gets Migrated?
    64→
    65→**Everything!** All your data:
    66→
    67→- ✅ **Tasks** (Kanban boards, statuses, descriptions)
    68→- ✅ **Calendar events** (dates, titles, descriptions)
    69→- ✅ **Habits & gamification** (streaks, XP, levels)
    70→- ✅ **Time tracking** (entries, durations, projects)
    71→- ✅ **Theme preferences** (dark mode, colors)
    72→- ✅ **Weather data, maps, resources, risks, Gantt charts**
    73→- ✅ **System metrics & incidents**
    74→- 🔜 **Notes** (when implemented Q1 2026)
    75→
    76→---
    77→
    78→## Is My Data Safe?
    79→
    80→**YES!** Migration is designed for safety:
    81→
    82→### During Migration:
    83→✅ **Data is copied, not moved** - Original localStorage data stays intact
    84→✅ **No data sent to servers** - Everything happens locally in your browser
    85→✅ **Validates data integrity** - Ensures data isn't corrupted
    86→✅ **Can be retried** - If migration fails, you can try again
    87→✅ **Automatic fallback** - If IndexedDB fails, app uses localStorage
    88→
    89→### After Migration:
    90→✅ **Both storage systems work together** - Data is read from IndexedDB first, localStorage as fallback
    91→✅ **No data loss** - Original localStorage data preserved
    92→✅ **Easy export** - Export .brain backup file anytime (Settings → Backup tab)
    93→
    94→---
    95→
    96→## How to Check Storage Usage
    97→
    98→Want to see how much space you're using?
    99→
   100→1. Go to **Settings** → **⚙️ Advanced**
   101→2. Look at **💾 Storage Information** section
   102→3. You'll see:
   103→   - **Progress bar** (green/yellow/red based on usage)
   104→   - **Storage used** (e.g., "1.5 MB / 50 GB")
   105→   - **Percentage used** (e.g., "0.03% used")
   106→   - **Available space** (e.g., "48.5 GB available")
   107→
   108→### Storage Warning ⚠️
   109→If you see a red warning banner:
   110→- You're using over 80% of available storage
   111→- Consider exporting old data as .brain files
   112→- Delete unused tasks, events, or notes (when available)
   113→
   114→---
   115→
   116→## Troubleshooting
   117→
   118→### Migration Failed - What Do I Do?
   119→
   120→**Option 1: Try Again**
   121→1. Go to **Settings** → **⚙️ Advanced**
   122→2. Click **🚀 Migrate to IndexedDB Now** again
   123→3. Migration is safe to run multiple times
   124→
   125→**Option 2: Export Backup First**
   126→If you're worried about data loss:
   127→1. Go to **Settings** → **💾 Backup & Restore**
   128→2. Click **📦 Export Brain** to create a backup
   129→3. Then try migration again
   130→
   131→**Option 3: Check Browser Compatibility**
   132→- **Chrome/Edge:** IndexedDB fully supported ✅
   133→- **Firefox:** IndexedDB fully supported ✅
   134→- **Safari:** IndexedDB fully supported ✅
   135→- **Private/Incognito:** IndexedDB may be disabled ⚠️
   136→
   137→### Can I Undo Migration?
   138→
   139→**Yes!** If you need to revert (rare):
   140→
   141→1. Open **Browser DevTools** (F12 or right-click → Inspect)
   142→2. Go to **Application** tab
   143→3. Find **IndexedDB** → **neuman-brain-db**
   144→4. Right-click → **Delete database**
   145→5. Refresh page - app will use localStorage again
   146→
   147→**Note:** This deletes IndexedDB data, but localStorage data is still there!
   148→
   149→### My Data Disappeared!
   150→
   151→**Don't panic!** Your data is likely safe:
   152→
   153→1. Check **Settings** → **⚙️ Advanced** → **Storage Information**
   154→2. Look at **Stored Items** count - if it's not zero, your data exists
   155→3. Try **exporting a .brain file** - if export succeeds, data is fine
   156→4. If export shows 0 items:
   157→   - Check if you're in the same browser (Chrome vs Firefox have separate storage)
   158→   - Check if you're in Private/Incognito mode (separate storage)
   159→   - Check if browser data was cleared recently
   160→
   161→---
   162→
   163→## How Much Storage Do I Have?
   164→
   165→Typical storage limits by browser:
   166→
   167→| Browser | IndexedDB Capacity | Notes |
   168→|---------|-------------------|-------|
   169→| **Chrome** | 10GB - 50GB+ | Depends on available disk space |
   170→| **Firefox** | 10GB - 50GB+ | Asks permission for large storage |
   171→| **Safari** | 1GB - 10GB | More conservative limits |
   172→| **Edge** | 10GB - 50GB+ | Same as Chrome (Chromium-based) |
   173→
   174→**How to check:**
   175→- Go to **Settings** → **⚙️ Advanced**
   176→- See **Storage Information** for your specific limit
   177→
   178→---
   179→
   180→## Technical Details (For Curious Users)
   181→
   182→### What is IndexedDB?
   183→
   184→IndexedDB is a **low-level API for client-side storage** built into modern browsers:
   185→
   186→- **Structured database** (like a mini SQL database in your browser)
   187→- **Asynchronous** (doesn't freeze the UI when saving)
   188→- **Large capacity** (50GB+ vs 5-10MB for localStorage)
   189→- **Indexed queries** (fast lookups by key)
   190→- **Transactions** (atomic operations - all succeed or all fail)
   191→
   192→### What is localStorage?
   193→
   194→localStorage is an **older, simpler storage API**:
   195→
   196→- **Key-value store** (like a simple dictionary)
   197→- **Synchronous** (can freeze UI on large saves)
   198→- **Limited capacity** (5-10MB - too small for rich content)
   199→- **No indexing** (slower for large datasets)
   200→- **Simple API** (easy to use but limited)
   201→
   202→### Why Not Just Use localStorage?
   203→
   204→localStorage hits limits quickly:
   205→- 📷 **One large image** = 1-2MB (20-40% of storage!)
   206→- 📝 **Rich text notes** = 100KB-1MB each
   207→- 📊 **Large datasets** = Thousands of tasks/events
   208→
   209→IndexedDB solves these problems with 50GB+ capacity.
   210→
   211→---
   212→
   213→## Privacy & Security
   214→
   215→### Is My Data Private?
   216→
   217→**YES!** Your data never leaves your browser:
   218→
   219→✅ **No server uploads** - All data stays on your device
   220→✅ **No tracking** - We don't collect usage data
   221→✅ **No analytics** - Zero telemetry unless you opt-in
   222→✅ **Local-first** - Works 100% offline
   223→
   224→### When Will Data Be Encrypted?
   225→
   226→**Q2 2026:** End-to-end encryption (E2EE) for .brain backup files:
   227→- Export .brain files will be encrypted with AES-256-GCM
   228→- User-controlled passphrases (we never see them)
   229→- Zero-knowledge architecture (we can't read your data)
   230→
   231→**Today:** Data is stored unencrypted in IndexedDB (browser handles disk encryption if enabled)
   232→
   233→---
   234→
   235→## FAQ
   236→
   237→**Q: Will migration delete my data?**
   238→A: No! Data is copied, not moved. Original localStorage data stays intact.
   239→
   240→**Q: How long does migration take?**
   241→A: Usually 5-10 seconds. Depends on data size (typical: 10-50 items = 2 seconds).
   242→
   243→**Q: Can I use Neuman Brain during migration?**
   244→A: Yes! Migration happens in the background. You can keep working.
   245→
   246→**Q: What if my browser doesn't support IndexedDB?**
   247→A: App automatically falls back to localStorage. You'll see a warning.
   248→
   249→**Q: Will migration use my mobile data?**
   250→A: No! Migration is 100% local. Zero network usage.
   251→
   252→**Q: Can I migrate multiple times?**
   253→A: Yes! Migration is idempotent (safe to run multiple times). Already-migrated data is skipped.
   254→
   255→**Q: Will this break my existing backups?**
   256→A: No! .brain export/import works the same regardless of storage type.
   257→
   258→**Q: Do I need to migrate on every device?**
   259→A: Yes. Each browser/device has separate storage. Migration is per-browser.
   260→
   261→**Q: What happens if I clear browser data?**
   262→A: Both localStorage AND IndexedDB are cleared. Always export .brain backups!
   263→
   264→---
   265→
   266→## Need Help?
   267→
   268→**Support Options:**
   269→
   270→1. **GitHub Issues:** https://github.com/travisjneuman/system.knowledgebase/issues
   271→2. **Export .brain file** before asking for help (Settings → Backup)
   272→3. **Include browser info** (Chrome 120, Firefox 121, etc.)
   273→4. **Include error messages** (check browser console: F12 → Console)
   274→
   275→---
   276→
   277→## Summary
   278→
   279→🎉 **Good news:** IndexedDB migration makes Neuman Brain better!
   280→
   281→✅ **Automatic:** Migration happens without user action
   282→✅ **Safe:** Data is copied, not deleted
   283→✅ **Fast:** Takes 5-10 seconds
   284→✅ **Private:** Zero network usage, 100% local
   285→✅ **Future-proof:** Room for notes, images, and more
   286→
   287→**Most users won't even notice** - it just works! 🚀
   288→
   289→If you want to check status or manually trigger migration:
   290→- **Settings** → **⚙️ Advanced** → **💾 Storage Information**
   291→
   292→---
   293→
   294→**Questions?** Check the FAQ above or create a GitHub issue!
   295→

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

# Storage Migration Guide

**Last Updated:** November 13, 2025
**Audience:** Neuman Brain users
**Purpose:** Understand storage upgrades and data migration

---

## What Changed?

Neuman Brain has upgraded from **localStorage** (5-10MB limit) to **IndexedDB** (50GB+ capacity) to support:

✅ **Rich text notes with images** (coming in Q1 2026)
✅ **Large datasets** (thousands of tasks, events, and notes)
✅ **Better performance** (async operations don't block the UI)
✅ **Offline support** (works without internet)
✅ **Future-proof** (room for growth as you add more data)

---

## Do I Need to Do Anything?

**No! Migration happens automatically** when you:

1. Open Neuman Brain in your browser
2. Use any feature (tasks, calendar, habits, etc.)
3. Data is automatically copied from localStorage to IndexedDB

You don't need to lift a finger - it just works! 🎉

---

## Manual Migration (Optional)

If you want to migrate everything at once or check migration status:

### Step-by-Step Instructions

1. Open **Neuman Brain** in your browser
2. Click **Settings** (gear icon in navigation)
3. Go to **⚙️ Advanced** tab
4. Scroll to **💾 Storage Information** section
5. You'll see one of three states:

#### State 1: Migration Needed ⚠️
If you see "Migration Status: ⚠️ Needed":
- Click **🚀 Migrate to IndexedDB Now** button
- Wait 5-10 seconds (depends on data size)
- You'll see: "✅ Migration successful! Migrated X items"

#### State 2: Migration Complete ✅
If you see "Migration Status: ✅ Complete":
- You're all set! Nothing to do.
- Your data is already in IndexedDB.

#### State 3: Not Needed ✅
If you see "Migration Status: ✅ Not Needed":
- This is a fresh install or you've never used localStorage
- All new data automatically goes to IndexedDB

---

## What Gets Migrated?

**Everything!** All your data:

- ✅ **Tasks** (Kanban boards, statuses, descriptions)
- ✅ **Calendar events** (dates, titles, descriptions)
- ✅ **Habits & gamification** (streaks, XP, levels)
- ✅ **Time tracking** (entries, durations, projects)
- ✅ **Theme preferences** (dark mode, colors)
- ✅ **Weather data, maps, resources, risks, Gantt charts**
- ✅ **System metrics & incidents**
- 🔜 **Notes** (when implemented Q1 2026)

---

## Is My Data Safe?

**YES!** Migration is designed for safety:

### During Migration:
✅ **Data is copied, not moved** - Original localStorage data stays intact
✅ **No data sent to servers** - Everything happens locally in your browser
✅ **Validates data integrity** - Ensures data isn't corrupted
✅ **Can be retried** - If migration fails, you can try again
✅ **Automatic fallback** - If IndexedDB fails, app uses localStorage

### After Migration:
✅ **Both storage systems work together** - Data is read from IndexedDB first, localStorage as fallback
✅ **No data loss** - Original localStorage data preserved
✅ **Easy export** - Export .brain backup file anytime (Settings → Backup tab)

---

## How to Check Storage Usage

Want to see how much space you're using?

1. Go to **Settings** → **⚙️ Advanced**
2. Look at **💾 Storage Information** section
3. You'll see:
   - **Progress bar** (green/yellow/red based on usage)
   - **Storage used** (e.g., "1.5 MB / 50 GB")
   - **Percentage used** (e.g., "0.03% used")
   - **Available space** (e.g., "48.5 GB available")

### Storage Warning ⚠️
If you see a red warning banner:
- You're using over 80% of available storage
- Consider exporting old data as .brain files
- Delete unused tasks, events, or notes (when available)

---

## Troubleshooting

### Migration Failed - What Do I Do?

**Option 1: Try Again**
1. Go to **Settings** → **⚙️ Advanced**
2. Click **🚀 Migrate to IndexedDB Now** again
3. Migration is safe to run multiple times

**Option 2: Export Backup First**
If you're worried about data loss:
1. Go to **Settings** → **💾 Backup & Restore**
2. Click **📦 Export Brain** to create a backup
3. Then try migration again

**Option 3: Check Browser Compatibility**
- **Chrome/Edge:** IndexedDB fully supported ✅
- **Firefox:** IndexedDB fully supported ✅
- **Safari:** IndexedDB fully supported ✅
- **Private/Incognito:** IndexedDB may be disabled ⚠️

### Can I Undo Migration?

**Yes!** If you need to revert (rare):

1. Open **Browser DevTools** (F12 or right-click → Inspect)
2. Go to **Application** tab
3. Find **IndexedDB** → **neuman-brain-db**
4. Right-click → **Delete database**
5. Refresh page - app will use localStorage again

**Note:** This deletes IndexedDB data, but localStorage data is still there!

### My Data Disappeared!

**Don't panic!** Your data is likely safe:

1. Check **Settings** → **⚙️ Advanced** → **Storage Information**
2. Look at **Stored Items** count - if it's not zero, your data exists
3. Try **exporting a .brain file** - if export succeeds, data is fine
4. If export shows 0 items:
   - Check if you're in the same browser (Chrome vs Firefox have separate storage)
   - Check if you're in Private/Incognito mode (separate storage)
   - Check if browser data was cleared recently

---

## How Much Storage Do I Have?

Typical storage limits by browser:

| Browser | IndexedDB Capacity | Notes |
|---------|-------------------|-------|
| **Chrome** | 10GB - 50GB+ | Depends on available disk space |
| **Firefox** | 10GB - 50GB+ | Asks permission for large storage |
| **Safari** | 1GB - 10GB | More conservative limits |
| **Edge** | 10GB - 50GB+ | Same as Chrome (Chromium-based) |

**How to check:**
- Go to **Settings** → **⚙️ Advanced**
- See **Storage Information** for your specific limit

---

## Technical Details (For Curious Users)

### What is IndexedDB?

IndexedDB is a **low-level API for client-side storage** built into modern browsers:

- **Structured database** (like a mini SQL database in your browser)
- **Asynchronous** (doesn't freeze the UI when saving)
- **Large capacity** (50GB+ vs 5-10MB for localStorage)
- **Indexed queries** (fast lookups by key)
- **Transactions** (atomic operations - all succeed or all fail)

### What is localStorage?

localStorage is an **older, simpler storage API**:

- **Key-value store** (like a simple dictionary)
- **Synchronous** (can freeze UI on large saves)
- **Limited capacity** (5-10MB - too small for rich content)
- **No indexing** (slower for large datasets)
- **Simple API** (easy to use but limited)

### Why Not Just Use localStorage?

localStorage hits limits quickly:
- 📷 **One large image** = 1-2MB (20-40% of storage!)
- 📝 **Rich text notes** = 100KB-1MB each
- 📊 **Large datasets** = Thousands of tasks/events

IndexedDB solves these problems with 50GB+ capacity.

---

## Privacy & Security

### Is My Data Private?

**YES!** Your data never leaves your browser:

✅ **No server uploads** - All data stays on your device
✅ **No tracking** - We don't collect usage data
✅ **No analytics** - Zero telemetry unless you opt-in
✅ **Local-first** - Works 100% offline

### When Will Data Be Encrypted?

**Q2 2026:** End-to-end encryption (E2EE) for .brain backup files:
- Export .brain files will be encrypted with AES-256-GCM
- User-controlled passphrases (we never see them)
- Zero-knowledge architecture (we can't read your data)

**Today:** Data is stored unencrypted in IndexedDB (browser handles disk encryption if enabled)

---

## FAQ

**Q: Will migration delete my data?**
A: No! Data is copied, not moved. Original localStorage data stays intact.

**Q: How long does migration take?**
A: Usually 5-10 seconds. Depends on data size (typical: 10-50 items = 2 seconds).

**Q: Can I use Neuman Brain during migration?**
A: Yes! Migration happens in the background. You can keep working.

**Q: What if my browser doesn't support IndexedDB?**
A: App automatically falls back to localStorage. You'll see a warning.

**Q: Will migration use my mobile data?**
A: No! Migration is 100% local. Zero network usage.

**Q: Can I migrate multiple times?**
A: Yes! Migration is idempotent (safe to run multiple times). Already-migrated data is skipped.

**Q: Will this break my existing backups?**
A: No! .brain export/import works the same regardless of storage type.

**Q: Do I need to migrate on every device?**
A: Yes. Each browser/device has separate storage. Migration is per-browser.

**Q: What happens if I clear browser data?**
A: Both localStorage AND IndexedDB are cleared. Always export .brain backups!

---

## Need Help?

**Support Options:**

1. **GitHub Issues:** https://github.com/travisjneuman/system.knowledgebase/issues
2. **Export .brain file** before asking for help (Settings → Backup)
3. **Include browser info** (Chrome 120, Firefox 121, etc.)
4. **Include error messages** (check browser console: F12 → Console)

---

## Summary

🎉 **Good news:** IndexedDB migration makes Neuman Brain better!

✅ **Automatic:** Migration happens without user action
✅ **Safe:** Data is copied, not deleted
✅ **Fast:** Takes 5-10 seconds
✅ **Private:** Zero network usage, 100% local
✅ **Future-proof:** Room for notes, images, and more

**Most users won't even notice** - it just works! 🚀

If you want to check status or manually trigger migration:
- **Settings** → **⚙️ Advanced** → **💾 Storage Information**

---

**Questions?** Check the FAQ above or create a GitHub issue!

   456→- Only you know the password
   457→
   458→**⚠️ Risky if:**
   459→- Weak password
   460→- File shared/leaked
   461→- Others have access
   462→
   463→**Default:** Disabled (keys NOT exported)
   464→
   465→**To enable:**
   466→1. Go to AI Terminal Settings
   467→2. Look for "Export Encrypted Keys"
   468→3. Enable the toggle
   469→4. Your encrypted keys will be included in .brain exports
   470→
   471→---
   472→
   473→## Tips & Best Practices
   474→
   475→### Getting Better Results
   476→
   477→**Be specific:**
   478→- ❌ "Help with code"
   479→- ✅ "Write a React component that fetches data from an API and displays it in a table"
   480→
   481→**Provide context:**
   482→- Include error messages
   483→- Mention what you've tried
   484→- Share relevant code snippets
   485→
   486→**Use the right model:**
   487→- Quick questions → Fast models
   488→- Complex reasoning → Quality models
   489→- Code generation → Code-focused models
   490→
   491→### Managing Costs
   492→
   493→**Use free providers first:**
   494→- OpenRouter, Groq, HuggingFace are excellent and free!
   495→
   496→**Monitor usage:**
   497→- Check the Usage Tracker regularly
   498→- Set up multiple providers for redundancy
   499→- Use cheaper models for simple tasks
   500→
   501→**Estimated costs** (paid providers):
   502→- GPT-4o Mini: $0.15 per 1M tokens (~6,000 messages)
   503→- Claude 3.5 Haiku: $1 per 1M tokens (~1,000 messages)
   504→- GPT-4o: $2.50 per 1M tokens (~400 messages)
   505→
   506→### Security Best Practices
   507→
   508→1. **Use strong passwords** - 16+ characters with mix of types
   509→2. **Don't share .brain files** - Especially with encrypted keys enabled
   510→3. **Rotate API keys** - Change them every few months
   511→4. **Monitor API usage** - Check provider dashboards for suspicious activity
   512→5. **Use free tiers first** - Test before committing to paid services
   513→
   514→---
   515→
   516→## Troubleshooting
   517→
   518→### "Provider not configured"
   519→
   520→**Problem:** You haven't added an API key for this provider.
   521→
   522→**Solution:**
   523→1. Click ⚙️ **Settings** in AI Terminal header
   524→2. Find the provider you want to use
   525→3. Click **Configure** or **Add API Key**
   526→4. Follow the provider-specific instructions above
   527→5. Click **Save** and then **Test Key** to verify
   528→6. Click **Done** to close settings
   529→
   530→---
   531→
   532→### "Invalid API key"
   533→
   534→**Problem:** The API key you entered is incorrect, expired, or revoked.
   535→
   536→**Common Causes:**
   537→- Typo when copying/pasting (extra space, missing character)
   538→- Wrong provider selected (e.g., using Groq key for OpenRouter)
   539→- API key revoked or deleted from provider dashboard
   540→- API key expired (some providers have expiry dates)
   541→- Payment method declined (for paid providers)
   542→
   543→**Solution:**
   544→1. **Double-check the key format:**
   545→   - OpenRouter: `sk-or-v1-...`
   546→   - Groq: `gsk_...`
   547→   - HuggingFace: `hf_...`
   548→   - OpenAI: `sk-...`
   549→   - Anthropic: `sk-ant-...`
   550→   - Gemini: `AIza...`
   551→2. **Copy/paste carefully** - Don't type it manually
   552→3. **Check provider dashboard:**
   553→   - Log in to the provider's website
   554→   - Navigate to API Keys section
   555→   - Verify the key still exists and is active
   556→4. **Regenerate key:**
   557→   - Delete old key in provider dashboard
   558→   - Create new API key
   559→   - Update it in Neuman Brain settings
   560→5. **For paid providers:** Verify payment method is valid
   561→
   562→---
   563→
   564→### "Failed to decrypt API key"
   565→
   566→**Problem:** Wrong password or corrupted encryption data.
   567→
   568→**Common Causes:**
   569→- Incorrect password (typo, caps lock, wrong keyboard layout)
   570→- Password changed since encryption
   571→- Browser storage corrupted
   572→- Different password used for different keys
   573→
   574→**Solution:**
   575→
   576→**Option 1: Try password again**
   577→1. Check **Caps Lock** is off
   578→2. Check keyboard layout (US vs international)
   579→3. Try typing password in a text editor first to verify
   580→4. Copy/paste password to ensure accuracy
   581→
   582→**Option 2: Reset encryption password**
   583→1. Click ⚙️ **Settings** in AI Terminal
   584→2. Scroll to **Security** section
   585→3. Click **Clear All Encrypted Keys** (⚠️ This removes ALL API keys!)
   586→4. Confirm deletion
   587→5. Re-add your API keys with new password
   588→6. Choose a password you'll remember (use password manager!)
   589→
   590→**Option 3: Check browser console for details**
   591→1. Open browser DevTools (F12)
   592→2. Go to **Console** tab
   593→3. Look for specific decryption error
   594→4. May indicate corrupted storage → Clear browser data
   595→
   596→**Prevention:**
   597→- Use a **password manager** (1Password, Bitwarden, LastPass)
   598→- Write password down securely (not on computer!)
   599→- Use consistent password across all keys
   600→
   601→---
   602→
   603→### "Rate limit exceeded"
   604→
   605→**Problem:** You've made too many requests to this provider in a short time.
   606→
   607→**Rate Limits by Provider:**
   608→- **OpenRouter Free:** 200 requests/minute
   609→- **Groq Free:** 30 requests/minute, 14,400 requests/day
   610→- **HuggingFace:** Varies by model (usually 1,000/day)
   611→- **Mistral Free:** ~100 requests/day
   612→- **Gemini Free:** 15 requests/minute (Flash), 2 requests/minute (Pro)
   613→- **OpenAI Tier 1:** 3,500 requests/day
   614→- **Anthropic Tier 1:** 50 requests/minute
   615→
   616→**Solution:**
   617→
   618→**Immediate:**
   619→1. **Wait** - Rate limits reset after time period (1 minute to 24 hours)
   620→2. **Automatic fallback** will switch to another provider
   621→3. **Check Usage Tracker** (📊) to see which provider has quota left
   622→
   623→**Long-term:**
   624→1. **Configure multiple providers:**
   625→   - Set up OpenRouter + Groq + HuggingFace (all free!)
   626→   - System automatically falls back when one hits limit
   627→2. **Upgrade to paid tier:**
   628→   - Paid providers have much higher limits (10,000+ requests/day)
   629→   - OpenRouter paid removes rate limits entirely
   630→3. **Batch your requests:**
   631→   - Instead of 10 quick questions, combine into one longer question
   632→   - Reduces API calls while getting same information
   633→4. **Use faster models:**
   634→   - Groq models respond in <1s, so you can ask 30 questions/minute vs 2/minute
   635→
   636→**Check remaining quota:**
   637→1. Visit provider dashboard (links above in "Getting API Keys")
   638→2. Look for "Usage" or "Billing" section
   639→3. See requests made today/this month
   640→
   641→---
   642→
   643→### "CORS error" (OpenAI, xAI)
   644→
   645→**Problem:** These providers don't allow direct browser API calls for security reasons.
   646→
   647→**Technical Details:**
   648→- CORS (Cross-Origin Resource Sharing) prevents browsers from calling certain APIs
   649→- OpenAI and xAI block browser requests to prevent API key theft
   650→- Anthropic, Google, OpenRouter DO support browser calls
   651→
   652→**Solution:**
   653→
   654→**Option 1: Use OpenRouter (RECOMMENDED)**
   655→- OpenRouter proxies to OpenAI models (GPT-4o, GPT-4o Mini, o1)
   656→- Works in browser with no CORS issues
   657→- Same models, same quality, easier to use
   658→- **Free tier available!**
   659→
   660→**Option 2: Use Anthropic Claude**
   661→- Claude 3.5 Sonnet is comparable to GPT-4o
   662→- Works directly in browser (CORS enabled)
   663→- Often considered higher quality for writing/analysis
   664→
   665→**Option 3: Wait for backend proxy**
   666→- We're planning a backend proxy service (Q2 2026)
   667→- Will enable direct OpenAI/xAI access from browser
   668→- Optional feature (privacy-preserving)
   669→
   670→**Not recommended:**
   671→- ❌ Browser extensions (security risk)
   672→- ❌ CORS unblocker extensions (breaks other sites)
   673→- ❌ Running local proxy (complex setup)
   674→
   675→---
   676→
   677→### Password Prompt Every Time
   678→
   679→**Problem:** You're being asked to enter your encryption password every session.
   680→
   681→**Why This Happens:**
   682→- Password expired (based on expiry setting: daily, weekly, monthly)
   683→- Browser session cleared (closed browser, cleared cookies)
   684→- Browser privacy mode (password never persists)
   685→- Different browser/device
   686→
   687→**Solution:**
   688→
   689→**Option 1: Change expiry duration**
   690→1. Next time you enter password, look for "Remember for" dropdown
   691→2. Change from **Daily** to **Weekly** or **Monthly**
   692→3. Trade-off: Longer duration = less secure but more convenient
   693→
   694→**Option 2: Use browser password manager**
   695→1. When prompted for password, let browser save it
   696→2. Browser will auto-fill on next prompt
   697→3. Still secure (browser encrypts saved passwords)
   698→
   699→**Option 3: Accept it (most secure)**
   700→- Daily re-entry is the MOST secure option
   701→- Prevents unauthorized access if device stolen
   702→- Recommended for sensitive API keys
   703→
   704→**Not recommended:**
   705→- ❌ Disabling encryption (API keys stored in plaintext!)
   706→- ❌ Using weak password for convenience (defeats encryption)
   707→
   708→---
   709→
   710→### Slow Responses
   711→
   712→**Problem:** AI responses taking 10+ seconds to appear.
   713→
   714→**Common Causes:**
   715→- Using slow model (quality rating high, speed rating low)
   716→- Large context (long conversation history)
   717→- Provider experiencing high load
   718→- Network latency
   719→
   720→**Solution:**
   721→
   722→**Option 1: Switch to faster model**
   723→1. Click **🔀 Model Selector**
   724→2. Filter by **Speed** rating (4-5 stars)
   725→3. Select Groq Llama 3.3 or Gemini Flash
   726→4. Should see responses in <1 second
   727→
   728→**Option 2: Clear conversation history**
   729→1. Click **🗑️** trash icon to clear chat
   730→2. Shorter context = faster responses
   731→3. Long conversations increase processing time
   732→
   733→**Option 3: Try different provider**
   734→1. Check if other providers are faster right now
   735→2. OpenRouter → Groq → HuggingFace
   736→3. Some providers have peak/off-peak hours
   737→
   738→**Speed Comparison:**
   739→- **Groq:** <1 second (custom LPU hardware)
   740→- **Gemini Flash:** 1-3 seconds
   741→- **Claude 3.5 Haiku:** 2-4 seconds
   742→- **GPT-4o Mini:** 3-5 seconds
   743→- **Claude 3.5 Sonnet:** 5-8 seconds
   744→- **GPT-4o:** 8-12 seconds
   745→- **o1:** 30-60 seconds (reasoning model)
   746→
   747→---
   748→
   749→### "Network error" or "Failed to fetch"
   750→
   751→**Problem:** Cannot connect to AI provider.
   752→
   753→**Common Causes:**
   754→- Internet connection down
   755→- Provider API server down
   756→- Firewall blocking API calls
   757→- VPN interfering with connection
   758→
   759→**Solution:**
   760→
   761→**Option 1: Check internet connection**
   762→1. Open another website (google.com)
   763→2. If it loads, internet is working
   764→3. If not, fix internet connection first
   765→
   766→**Option 2: Check provider status**
   767→1. Visit provider status page:
   768→   - OpenRouter: [status.openrouter.ai](https://status.openrouter.ai)
   769→   - Groq: [status.groq.com](https://status.groq.com)
   770→   - OpenAI: [status.openai.com](https://status.openai.com)
   771→   - Anthropic: [status.anthropic.com](https://status.anthropic.com)
   772→2. If provider is down, automatic fallback will activate
   773→3. Or manually switch to another provider
   774→
   775→**Option 3: Disable VPN temporarily**
   776→1. Some VPNs block AI API endpoints
   777→2. Try disabling VPN
   778→3. If it works, whitelist the AI provider domains
   779→
   780→**Option 4: Check browser console**
   781→1. Open DevTools (F12) → Console tab
   782→2. Look for specific error message
   783→3. May indicate firewall or network policy blocking
   784→
   785→**Option 5: Try different browser**
   786→- Some corporate firewalls block in specific browsers
   787→- Try Chrome, Firefox, Edge, Safari
   788→
   789→---
   790→
   791→### Responses in Wrong Language
   792→
   793→**Problem:** AI responding in a language you didn't request.
   794→
   795→**Common Causes:**
   796→- Model defaulting to its training language
   797→- Previous conversation context
   798→- Browser language settings
   799→
   800→**Solution:**
   801→
   802→**Option 1: Explicitly specify language**
   803→```
   804→Please respond in English.
   805→[Your question here]
   806→```
   807→
   808→**Option 2: Use English-first models**
   809→- Llama 3.3 (English-first)
   810→- GPT-4o (English-first)
   811→- Claude (English-first)
   812→- Avoid: Qwen (Chinese-first), Mistral (French-first)
   813→
   814→**Option 3: Clear conversation and restart**
   815→1. Click 🗑️ to clear chat history
   816→2. Start fresh conversation in desired language
   817→3. Model won't have conflicting context
   818→
   819→---
   820→
   821→### API Key Compromise / Unauthorized Usage
   822→
   823→**Problem:** You suspect your API key has been stolen or leaked.
   824→
   825→**Immediate Actions:**
   826→1. **Revoke key immediately** in provider dashboard
   827→2. **Generate new key**
   828→3. **Check usage/billing** in provider dashboard for suspicious activity
   829→4. **Report to provider** if unauthorized charges occurred
   830→5. **Update key** in Neuman Brain settings
   831→
   832→**Prevention:**
   833→- ✅ Never commit API keys to GitHub/GitLab
   834→- ✅ Never share .brain files with encrypted keys enabled
   835→- ✅ Use strong encryption password (16+ characters)
   836→- ✅ Rotate keys every 3-6 months
   837→- ✅ Monitor provider dashboards for unusual activity
   838→- ✅ Set spending limits in provider dashboards (if available)
   839→
   840→**Spending Limits:**
   841→- OpenAI: Settings → Billing → Usage limits
   842→- Anthropic: Settings → Billing → Set budget
   843→- Google: Cloud Console → Billing → Budgets
   844→
   845→---
   846→
   847→## Keyboard Shortcuts
   848→
   849→- **Enter** - Send message
   850→- **Shift+Enter** - New line in message
   851→- **Esc** - Close modal/panel (when focused)
   852→
   853→---
   854→
   855→## FAQs
   856→
   857→### Is my data private?
   858→
   859→**Yes!** Everything is stored locally in your browser:
   860→- Chat history → Browser storage
   861→- API keys → Encrypted in browser storage
   862→- No cloud storage, no server tracking
   863→
   864→### Can I use multiple providers at once?
   865→
   866→**Yes!** Configure as many providers as you want. The system uses one at a time but automatically falls back if needed.
   867→
   868→### What happens if I forget my password?
   869→
   870→You'll need to clear your API keys and re-add them with a new password. Your chat history is NOT affected.
   871→
   872→### How do I remove a provider?
   873→
   874→1. Open AI Terminal
   875→2. Click ⚙️ Settings
   876→3. Find the provider
   877→4. Click **Remove** button
   878→
   879→### Can I export my chat history?
   880→
   881→Yes! Use the .brain export feature in Settings. Your entire chat history is included.
   882→
   883→### Which provider is best?
   884→
   885→**For free:** OpenRouter (most versatile) or Groq (fastest)
   886→**For paid:** Claude 3.5 Sonnet (best quality) or GPT-4o (most popular)
   887→
   888→### Do I need to pay for AI providers?
   889→
   890→No! 4 providers offer free tiers:
   891→- OpenRouter
   892→- Groq
   893→- HuggingFace
   894→- Mistral
   895→
   896→### How much do paid providers cost?
   897→
   898→Typically $0.15-$15 per 1 million tokens. For reference:
   899→- 1M tokens ≈ 750,000 words
   900→- Average conversation: 500-2000 tokens
   901→- You can have hundreds/thousands of conversations for $1
   902→
   903→---
   904→
   905→## Getting Help
   906→
   907→### Provider Issues
   908→
   909→Each provider has their own support:
   910→- **OpenRouter:** [openrouter.ai/docs](https://openrouter.ai/docs)
   911→- **Groq:** [console.groq.com/docs](https://console.groq.com/docs)
   912→- **HuggingFace:** [huggingface.co/docs](https://huggingface.co/docs)
   913→- **Mistral:** [docs.mistral.ai](https://docs.mistral.ai)
   914→- **OpenAI:** [platform.openai.com/docs](https://platform.openai.com/docs)
   915→- **Anthropic:** [docs.anthropic.com](https://docs.anthropic.com)
   916→- **Gemini:** [ai.google.dev/docs](https://ai.google.dev/docs)
   917→- **xAI:** [docs.x.ai](https://docs.x.ai)
   918→
   919→### Technical Issues
   920→
   921→For bugs or feature requests, please file an issue on our GitHub repository.
   922→
   923→---
   924→
   925→## Changelog
   926→
   927→### Version 2.0 (November 2025)
   928→- ✅ Multi-provider support (8 providers)
   929→- ✅ Encrypted API key storage
   930→- ✅ Automatic fallback system
   931→- ✅ Usage tracking per provider
   932→- ✅ Model selector with filtering
   933→- ✅ Free tier support
   934→
   935→### Version 1.0 (Previous)
   936→- Single provider (Google Gemini)
   937→- Plaintext API key storage
   938→- No fallback
   939→
   940→---
   941→
   942→**Enjoy your AI Terminal!** 🚀
   943→
   944→If you have questions or feedback, we'd love to hear from you.
   945→

<system-reminder>
