# AI Terminal User Guide

**Welcome to the Multi-Provider AI Terminal!**

Get help with coding, questions, productivity, and more using 8 different AI providers with automatic fallback and encrypted key storage.

---

## Quick Start

### Step 1: Open the AI Terminal

Click the **🤖 AI Terminal** button in the bottom-right corner of the screen.

### Step 2: Configure a Provider

1. Click the **⚙️ Settings** button in the terminal header
2. Choose a provider (see recommendations below)
3. Get an API key from the provider's website
4. Enter your API key and click **Save**
5. Click **Test Key** to verify it works
6. Click **Done** to close settings

### Step 3: Start Chatting!

Type your question or request and press Enter. The AI will respond in real-time!

---

## Choosing a Provider

### Cost Comparison Table

| Provider | Free Tier | Paid Pricing | Credit Card Required | Best For |
|----------|-----------|--------------|---------------------|----------|
| **OpenRouter** | ✅ Many free models | $0.06-$30/1M tokens | ❌ No | Most versatile |
| **Groq** | ✅ Generous free tier | N/A (free only) | ❌ No | Fastest speed |
| **HuggingFace** | ✅ True free tier | N/A (free only) | ❌ No | Open source |
| **Mistral** | ⚠️ Limited free | $0.25-$2/1M tokens | ⚠️ Yes | GDPR compliance |
| **Gemini** | ✅ Free tier | $0.075-$1.25/1M tokens | ⚠️ Yes | Large context |
| **OpenAI** | ❌ No | $0.15-$15/1M tokens | ✅ Yes | GPT-4o, o1 |
| **Anthropic** | ❌ No | $1-$15/1M tokens | ✅ Yes | Best quality |
| **xAI** | ❌ No | ~$5/1M tokens | ✅ Yes | Real-time info |

**💡 Tip:** Start with OpenRouter or Groq (both free, no credit card needed)

---

## Getting API Keys: Step-by-Step

### 🆓 Free Providers (Recommended to Start)

#### OpenRouter

**Why Choose:** Access to 200+ models (including GPT-4o, Claude, Llama) through one API. Many free models available.

**Step-by-Step:**
1. Go to [openrouter.ai](https://openrouter.ai)
2. Click **Sign In** (top right) → Sign in with Google/GitHub/Email
3. Once logged in, go to [openrouter.ai/keys](https://openrouter.ai/keys)
4. Click **Create Key** button
5. Give it a name (e.g., "Neuman Brain Dashboard")
6. Copy the API key (starts with `sk-or-v1-...`)
7. Paste into Neuman Brain AI Terminal settings

**Free Models Available:**
- Llama 3.3 70B Instruct (quality + free)
- Gemini 2.0 Flash Thinking (Google's latest, free)
- Mistral 7B (fast, free)
- Qwen 2.5 72B (multilingual, free)

**Rate Limits:** 200 requests/minute (generous!)

---

#### Groq

**Why Choose:** Fastest AI inference in the world (custom LPU hardware). 100% free.

**Step-by-Step:**
1. Go to [console.groq.com](https://console.groq.com)
2. Click **Sign Up** → Sign up with Google/Email
3. Verify your email (check inbox)
4. Once logged in, click **API Keys** in left sidebar
5. Click **Create API Key** button
6. Give it a name (e.g., "Neuman Brain")
7. Copy the API key (starts with `gsk_...`)
8. Paste into Neuman Brain AI Terminal settings

**Free Models Available:**
- Llama 3.3 70B (Meta's latest, free)
- Llama 3.1 8B (fast, free)
- Mixtral 8x7B (Mistral's MoE, free)
- Gemma 2 9B (Google, free)

**Rate Limits:** 30 requests/minute, 14,400 requests/day (very generous!)

**Speed:** Responses in <1 second (seriously!)

---

#### HuggingFace

**Why Choose:** Thousands of open-source models. True free tier with no credit card required.

**Step-by-Step:**
1. Go to [huggingface.co/join](https://huggingface.co/join)
2. Sign up with Email
3. Verify your email (check inbox)
4. Once logged in, go to [huggingface.co/settings/tokens](https://huggingface.co/settings/tokens)
5. Click **New token** button
6. Give it a name (e.g., "Neuman Brain Dashboard")
7. Select **Read** permission (sufficient for inference)
8. Click **Generate token**
9. Copy the token (starts with `hf_...`)
10. Paste into Neuman Brain AI Terminal settings

**Popular Models:**
- Meta Llama models (various sizes)
- Mistral models
- Falcon models
- Many community fine-tunes

**Rate Limits:** Varies by model, generally 1,000+ requests/day

---

#### Mistral

**Why Choose:** European AI company (GDPR-friendly). Privacy-focused.

**Step-by-Step:**
1. Go to [console.mistral.ai](https://console.mistral.ai)
2. Click **Sign Up** → Sign up with Google/Email
3. Verify your email
4. Once logged in, go to [console.mistral.ai/api-keys/](https://console.mistral.ai/api-keys/)
5. Click **Create new key** button
6. Give it a name (e.g., "Neuman Brain")
7. Copy the API key
8. Paste into Neuman Brain AI Terminal settings

**Models Available:**
- Mistral 7B (fast, free tier limited)
- Mixtral 8x7B (MoE, paid)
- Mistral Large (advanced, paid)

**Free Tier:** Limited free requests, then pay-as-you-go

**Pricing:** $0.25/1M tokens (Mistral 7B), $2/1M tokens (Mistral Large)

---

### 💳 Paid Providers (Bring Your Own Key)

#### Google Gemini

**Why Choose:** Massive 1M token context window. Multimodal (text + images).

**Step-by-Step:**
1. Go to [aistudio.google.com](https://aistudio.google.com)
2. Sign in with Google account
3. Click **Get API key** in left sidebar
4. Click **Create API key** button
5. Select a Google Cloud project (or create new one)
6. Copy the API key (starts with `AIza...`)
7. Paste into Neuman Brain AI Terminal settings

**Models Available:**
- Gemini 2.0 Flash Thinking (latest, fast, $0.075/1M tokens)
- Gemini 1.5 Pro (advanced, $1.25/1M tokens)
- Gemini 1.5 Flash (fast, $0.075/1M tokens)

**Free Tier:** 15 requests/minute, 1,500 requests/day (Flash), 2 requests/minute (Pro)

**Pricing:**
- Flash: $0.075/1M input tokens, $0.30/1M output tokens
- Pro: $1.25/1M input tokens, $5/1M output tokens

**Context Window:** Up to 1 million tokens (equivalent to 10 novels!)

---

#### OpenAI

**Why Choose:** GPT-4o (best coding model), o1 (best reasoning model).

**Step-by-Step:**
1. Go to [platform.openai.com/signup](https://platform.openai.com/signup)
2. Sign up with Email/Google/Microsoft
3. Verify your email
4. Add payment method (credit card required for API access)
5. Once logged in, go to [platform.openai.com/api-keys](https://platform.openai.com/api-keys)
6. Click **Create new secret key** button
7. Give it a name (e.g., "Neuman Brain Dashboard")
8. Copy the API key (starts with `sk-...`)
9. **Important:** Save it NOW (you can't view it again!)
10. Paste into Neuman Brain AI Terminal settings

**Models Available:**
- GPT-4o (best overall, $2.50/1M tokens)
- GPT-4o Mini (fast, cheap, $0.15/1M tokens)
- o1 (reasoning, $15/1M tokens)
- o1-mini (reasoning, cheaper, $3/1M tokens)

**Note:** OpenAI requires backend proxy due to CORS. Use OpenRouter to access OpenAI models instead!

**Pricing:**
- GPT-4o Mini: $0.15/1M input, $0.60/1M output
- GPT-4o: $2.50/1M input, $10/1M output
- o1: $15/1M input, $60/1M output

**Credit Required:** $5 minimum deposit

---

#### Anthropic Claude

**Why Choose:** Best quality responses for writing, analysis, coding. Works directly in browser!

**Step-by-Step:**
1. Go to [console.anthropic.com](https://console.anthropic.com)
2. Click **Sign Up** → Sign up with Email
3. Verify your email
4. Add payment method (credit card required)
5. Once logged in, go to [console.anthropic.com/settings/keys](https://console.anthropic.com/settings/keys)
6. Click **Create Key** button
7. Give it a name (e.g., "Neuman Brain")
8. Copy the API key (starts with `sk-ant-...`)
9. Paste into Neuman Brain AI Terminal settings

**Models Available:**
- Claude 3.5 Sonnet (best quality, $3/1M tokens)
- Claude 3.5 Haiku (fast, cheap, $1/1M tokens)
- Claude 3 Opus (legacy, expensive, $15/1M tokens)

**Pricing:**
- Haiku: $1/1M input, $5/1M output
- Sonnet: $3/1M input, $15/1M output
- Opus: $15/1M input, $75/1M output

**Credit Required:** $5 minimum deposit

**Special:** Claude works directly in browser (CORS enabled) unlike OpenAI!

---

#### xAI Grok

**Why Choose:** Unique personality, real-time information access (X/Twitter integration).

**Step-by-Step:**
1. Go to [console.x.ai](https://console.x.ai)
2. Sign in with X (Twitter) account
3. Navigate to API Keys section
4. Click **Create API Key**
5. Copy the API key
6. Paste into Neuman Brain AI Terminal settings

**Models Available:**
- Grok Beta (general purpose)
- Grok Vision (multimodal - coming soon)

**Note:** xAI requires backend proxy due to CORS limitations.

**Pricing:** Estimated $5/1M tokens (subject to change)

**Credit Required:** Payment details needed

---

## Understanding Models

### What Do the Ratings Mean?

**Speed Rating (1-5):**
- 1 = Slow (30+ seconds)
- 3 = Moderate (5-10 seconds)
- 5 = Lightning fast (<1 second)

**Quality Rating (1-5):**
- 1 = Basic responses
- 3 = Good for most tasks
- 5 = Excellent reasoning and accuracy

### Choosing the Right Model

**For Quick Questions:**
- Use **fast** models (speed rating 4-5)
- Examples: Groq Llama 3.3, Gemini Flash

**For Complex Tasks:**
- Use **quality** models (quality rating 4-5)
- Examples: Claude 3.5 Sonnet, GPT-4o, Llama 3.3 70B

**For Coding:**
- Good options: GPT-4o, Claude, Llama 3.3 70B
- Use cases: code generation, debugging, explanations

**For Analysis:**
- Best options: Claude 3.5 Sonnet, o1
- Use cases: research, writing, deep reasoning

**For Free Usage:**
- OpenRouter Llama 3.3 70B (quality + free)
- Groq models (speed + free)

---

### Best Model for Each Task Type

| Task Type | Best Free Model | Best Paid Model | Why |
|-----------|----------------|-----------------|-----|
| **Code Generation** | Llama 3.3 70B (OpenRouter) | GPT-4o | Strong programming knowledge |
| **Code Debugging** | Llama 3.3 70B (Groq) | Claude 3.5 Sonnet | Excellent error analysis |
| **Writing & Editing** | Llama 3.3 70B (OpenRouter) | Claude 3.5 Sonnet | Best prose quality |
| **Research & Analysis** | Qwen 2.5 72B (OpenRouter) | o1 | Deep reasoning |
| **Quick Q&A** | Gemini Flash (OpenRouter) | Claude 3.5 Haiku | Fast + accurate |
| **Brainstorming** | Mixtral 8x7B (Groq) | GPT-4o | Creative responses |
| **Math & Logic** | Llama 3.3 70B (Groq) | o1 | Step-by-step reasoning |
| **Multilingual** | Qwen 2.5 72B (OpenRouter) | Gemini Pro | 100+ languages |
| **Long Context** | Gemini Flash (free tier) | Gemini Pro | 1M token window |
| **Conversation** | Llama 3.3 70B (Groq) | Claude 3.5 Sonnet | Natural dialogue |

**Cost-Optimized Strategy:**
1. **Free Daily Use:** OpenRouter Llama 3.3 70B for everything
2. **Need Speed:** Groq Llama 3.3 70B (same model, 10x faster)
3. **Complex Reasoning:** Upgrade to Claude 3.5 Sonnet or o1 (only when needed)
4. **Huge Context:** Use Gemini Flash free tier (1M tokens)

---

## Using the AI Terminal

### Basic Chat

Just type your question and press Enter!

**Examples:**
```
You: What's the difference between React and Vue?

You: Write a function to validate email addresses in JavaScript

You: Help me debug this error: "Cannot read property 'map' of undefined"
```

### Streaming Responses

All providers support streaming (except o1 models), so you'll see responses appear in real-time!

### Multi-Line Input

- Press **Shift+Enter** for new line
- Press **Enter** alone to send message

### Clear Chat

Click the **🗑️** button to clear chat history.

---

## Advanced Features

### Model Selection

Click **🔀 Model Selector** to:
- Browse all available models
- Filter by provider, use case, or free/paid
- Compare speed, quality, context, and cost
- Switch models anytime

### Usage Tracking

Click **📊 Usage Tracker** to see:
- Requests per provider
- Token usage (estimated)
- Cost estimates (for paid providers)
- Time range filters (today, week, month, all)

### Automatic Fallback

If your primary provider fails (rate limit, quota, network error), the system automatically tries backup providers!

**You'll see a notification like:**
> ⚠️ openrouter failed: Rate limit exceeded. Switched to groq (llama-3.3-70b).

**Fallback order:**
1. OpenRouter
2. Groq
3. HuggingFace
4. Mistral
5. Gemini

### Provider Information

Each message shows which provider and model was used:

```
🤖 [Assistant Response]
12:34 PM • openrouter • llama-3.3-70b
```

---

## Encryption & Security

### Password Protection

Your API keys are encrypted with **AES-256-CBC** encryption!

**First Time Setup:**
1. When you save your first API key, you'll be prompted to create a password
2. Choose a **strong password** (min 12 characters)
3. Select password expiry (daily, weekly, or monthly)
4. Your password is stored **in memory only** (never saved to disk)

**Password Requirements:**
- Minimum 12 characters
- Mix of uppercase, lowercase, numbers, symbols
- Use a password manager!

**Password Expiry:**
- **Daily:** Re-enter password every 24 hours (most secure)
- **Weekly:** Re-enter every 7 days (recommended)
- **Monthly:** Re-enter every 30 days

### How Encryption Works

```
Your API Key
    ↓
Encrypted with your password (AES-256-CBC)
    ↓
Stored in browser (encrypted)
    ↓
Decrypted when needed (in memory only)
```

**Your API keys are NEVER:**
- ❌ Sent to our servers
- ❌ Stored in plaintext
- ❌ Shared with anyone

**Your password is NEVER:**
- ❌ Stored on disk
- ❌ Sent over network
- ❌ Recoverable if forgotten

### Backup with .brain Files

**Should you include encrypted keys in backups?**

**✅ Safe if:**
- You have a strong password
- File stored securely
- Only you know the password

**⚠️ Risky if:**
- Weak password
- File shared/leaked
- Others have access

**Default:** Disabled (keys NOT exported)

**To enable:**
1. Go to AI Terminal Settings
2. Look for "Export Encrypted Keys"
3. Enable the toggle
4. Your encrypted keys will be included in .brain exports

---

## Tips & Best Practices

### Getting Better Results

**Be specific:**
- ❌ "Help with code"
- ✅ "Write a React component that fetches data from an API and displays it in a table"

**Provide context:**
- Include error messages
- Mention what you've tried
- Share relevant code snippets

**Use the right model:**
- Quick questions → Fast models
- Complex reasoning → Quality models
- Code generation → Code-focused models

### Managing Costs

**Use free providers first:**
- OpenRouter, Groq, HuggingFace are excellent and free!

**Monitor usage:**
- Check the Usage Tracker regularly
- Set up multiple providers for redundancy
- Use cheaper models for simple tasks

**Estimated costs** (paid providers):
- GPT-4o Mini: $0.15 per 1M tokens (~6,000 messages)
- Claude 3.5 Haiku: $1 per 1M tokens (~1,000 messages)
- GPT-4o: $2.50 per 1M tokens (~400 messages)

### Security Best Practices

1. **Use strong passwords** - 16+ characters with mix of types
2. **Don't share .brain files** - Especially with encrypted keys enabled
3. **Rotate API keys** - Change them every few months
4. **Monitor API usage** - Check provider dashboards for suspicious activity
5. **Use free tiers first** - Test before committing to paid services

---

## Troubleshooting

### "Provider not configured"

**Problem:** You haven't added an API key for this provider.

**Solution:**
1. Click ⚙️ **Settings** in AI Terminal header
2. Find the provider you want to use
3. Click **Configure** or **Add API Key**
4. Follow the provider-specific instructions above
5. Click **Save** and then **Test Key** to verify
6. Click **Done** to close settings

---

### "Invalid API key"

**Problem:** The API key you entered is incorrect, expired, or revoked.

**Common Causes:**
- Typo when copying/pasting (extra space, missing character)
- Wrong provider selected (e.g., using Groq key for OpenRouter)
- API key revoked or deleted from provider dashboard
- API key expired (some providers have expiry dates)
- Payment method declined (for paid providers)

**Solution:**
1. **Double-check the key format:**
   - OpenRouter: `sk-or-v1-...`
   - Groq: `gsk_...`
   - HuggingFace: `hf_...`
   - OpenAI: `sk-...`
   - Anthropic: `sk-ant-...`
   - Gemini: `AIza...`
2. **Copy/paste carefully** - Don't type it manually
3. **Check provider dashboard:**
   - Log in to the provider's website
   - Navigate to API Keys section
   - Verify the key still exists and is active
4. **Regenerate key:**
   - Delete old key in provider dashboard
   - Create new API key
   - Update it in Neuman Brain settings
5. **For paid providers:** Verify payment method is valid

---

### "Failed to decrypt API key"

**Problem:** Wrong password or corrupted encryption data.

**Common Causes:**
- Incorrect password (typo, caps lock, wrong keyboard layout)
- Password changed since encryption
- Browser storage corrupted
- Different password used for different keys

**Solution:**

**Option 1: Try password again**
1. Check **Caps Lock** is off
2. Check keyboard layout (US vs international)
3. Try typing password in a text editor first to verify
4. Copy/paste password to ensure accuracy

**Option 2: Reset encryption password**
1. Click ⚙️ **Settings** in AI Terminal
2. Scroll to **Security** section
3. Click **Clear All Encrypted Keys** (⚠️ This removes ALL API keys!)
4. Confirm deletion
5. Re-add your API keys with new password
6. Choose a password you'll remember (use password manager!)

**Option 3: Check browser console for details**
1. Open browser DevTools (F12)
2. Go to **Console** tab
3. Look for specific decryption error
4. May indicate corrupted storage → Clear browser data

**Prevention:**
- Use a **password manager** (1Password, Bitwarden, LastPass)
- Write password down securely (not on computer!)
- Use consistent password across all keys

---

### "Rate limit exceeded"

**Problem:** You've made too many requests to this provider in a short time.

**Rate Limits by Provider:**
- **OpenRouter Free:** 200 requests/minute
- **Groq Free:** 30 requests/minute, 14,400 requests/day
- **HuggingFace:** Varies by model (usually 1,000/day)
- **Mistral Free:** ~100 requests/day
- **Gemini Free:** 15 requests/minute (Flash), 2 requests/minute (Pro)
- **OpenAI Tier 1:** 3,500 requests/day
- **Anthropic Tier 1:** 50 requests/minute

**Solution:**

**Immediate:**
1. **Wait** - Rate limits reset after time period (1 minute to 24 hours)
2. **Automatic fallback** will switch to another provider
3. **Check Usage Tracker** (📊) to see which provider has quota left

**Long-term:**
1. **Configure multiple providers:**
   - Set up OpenRouter + Groq + HuggingFace (all free!)
   - System automatically falls back when one hits limit
2. **Upgrade to paid tier:**
   - Paid providers have much higher limits (10,000+ requests/day)
   - OpenRouter paid removes rate limits entirely
3. **Batch your requests:**
   - Instead of 10 quick questions, combine into one longer question
   - Reduces API calls while getting same information
4. **Use faster models:**
   - Groq models respond in <1s, so you can ask 30 questions/minute vs 2/minute

**Check remaining quota:**
1. Visit provider dashboard (links above in "Getting API Keys")
2. Look for "Usage" or "Billing" section
3. See requests made today/this month

---

### "CORS error" (OpenAI, xAI)

**Problem:** These providers don't allow direct browser API calls for security reasons.

**Technical Details:**
- CORS (Cross-Origin Resource Sharing) prevents browsers from calling certain APIs
- OpenAI and xAI block browser requests to prevent API key theft
- Anthropic, Google, OpenRouter DO support browser calls

**Solution:**

**Option 1: Use OpenRouter (RECOMMENDED)**
- OpenRouter proxies to OpenAI models (GPT-4o, GPT-4o Mini, o1)
- Works in browser with no CORS issues
- Same models, same quality, easier to use
- **Free tier available!**

**Option 2: Use Anthropic Claude**
- Claude 3.5 Sonnet is comparable to GPT-4o
- Works directly in browser (CORS enabled)
- Often considered higher quality for writing/analysis

**Option 3: Wait for backend proxy**
- We're planning a backend proxy service (Q2 2026)
- Will enable direct OpenAI/xAI access from browser
- Optional feature (privacy-preserving)

**Not recommended:**
- ❌ Browser extensions (security risk)
- ❌ CORS unblocker extensions (breaks other sites)
- ❌ Running local proxy (complex setup)

---

### Password Prompt Every Time

**Problem:** You're being asked to enter your encryption password every session.

**Why This Happens:**
- Password expired (based on expiry setting: daily, weekly, monthly)
- Browser session cleared (closed browser, cleared cookies)
- Browser privacy mode (password never persists)
- Different browser/device

**Solution:**

**Option 1: Change expiry duration**
1. Next time you enter password, look for "Remember for" dropdown
2. Change from **Daily** to **Weekly** or **Monthly**
3. Trade-off: Longer duration = less secure but more convenient

**Option 2: Use browser password manager**
1. When prompted for password, let browser save it
2. Browser will auto-fill on next prompt
3. Still secure (browser encrypts saved passwords)

**Option 3: Accept it (most secure)**
- Daily re-entry is the MOST secure option
- Prevents unauthorized access if device stolen
- Recommended for sensitive API keys

**Not recommended:**
- ❌ Disabling encryption (API keys stored in plaintext!)
- ❌ Using weak password for convenience (defeats encryption)

---

### Slow Responses

**Problem:** AI responses taking 10+ seconds to appear.

**Common Causes:**
- Using slow model (quality rating high, speed rating low)
- Large context (long conversation history)
- Provider experiencing high load
- Network latency

**Solution:**

**Option 1: Switch to faster model**
1. Click **🔀 Model Selector**
2. Filter by **Speed** rating (4-5 stars)
3. Select Groq Llama 3.3 or Gemini Flash
4. Should see responses in <1 second

**Option 2: Clear conversation history**
1. Click **🗑️** trash icon to clear chat
2. Shorter context = faster responses
3. Long conversations increase processing time

**Option 3: Try different provider**
1. Check if other providers are faster right now
2. OpenRouter → Groq → HuggingFace
3. Some providers have peak/off-peak hours

**Speed Comparison:**
- **Groq:** <1 second (custom LPU hardware)
- **Gemini Flash:** 1-3 seconds
- **Claude 3.5 Haiku:** 2-4 seconds
- **GPT-4o Mini:** 3-5 seconds
- **Claude 3.5 Sonnet:** 5-8 seconds
- **GPT-4o:** 8-12 seconds
- **o1:** 30-60 seconds (reasoning model)

---

### "Network error" or "Failed to fetch"

**Problem:** Cannot connect to AI provider.

**Common Causes:**
- Internet connection down
- Provider API server down
- Firewall blocking API calls
- VPN interfering with connection

**Solution:**

**Option 1: Check internet connection**
1. Open another website (google.com)
2. If it loads, internet is working
3. If not, fix internet connection first

**Option 2: Check provider status**
1. Visit provider status page:
   - OpenRouter: [status.openrouter.ai](https://status.openrouter.ai)
   - Groq: [status.groq.com](https://status.groq.com)
   - OpenAI: [status.openai.com](https://status.openai.com)
   - Anthropic: [status.anthropic.com](https://status.anthropic.com)
2. If provider is down, automatic fallback will activate
3. Or manually switch to another provider

**Option 3: Disable VPN temporarily**
1. Some VPNs block AI API endpoints
2. Try disabling VPN
3. If it works, whitelist the AI provider domains

**Option 4: Check browser console**
1. Open DevTools (F12) → Console tab
2. Look for specific error message
3. May indicate firewall or network policy blocking

**Option 5: Try different browser**
- Some corporate firewalls block in specific browsers
- Try Chrome, Firefox, Edge, Safari

---

### Responses in Wrong Language

**Problem:** AI responding in a language you didn't request.

**Common Causes:**
- Model defaulting to its training language
- Previous conversation context
- Browser language settings

**Solution:**

**Option 1: Explicitly specify language**
```
Please respond in English.
[Your question here]
```

**Option 2: Use English-first models**
- Llama 3.3 (English-first)
- GPT-4o (English-first)
- Claude (English-first)
- Avoid: Qwen (Chinese-first), Mistral (French-first)

**Option 3: Clear conversation and restart**
1. Click 🗑️ to clear chat history
2. Start fresh conversation in desired language
3. Model won't have conflicting context

---

### API Key Compromise / Unauthorized Usage

**Problem:** You suspect your API key has been stolen or leaked.

**Immediate Actions:**
1. **Revoke key immediately** in provider dashboard
2. **Generate new key**
3. **Check usage/billing** in provider dashboard for suspicious activity
4. **Report to provider** if unauthorized charges occurred
5. **Update key** in Neuman Brain settings

**Prevention:**
- ✅ Never commit API keys to GitHub/GitLab
- ✅ Never share .brain files with encrypted keys enabled
- ✅ Use strong encryption password (16+ characters)
- ✅ Rotate keys every 3-6 months
- ✅ Monitor provider dashboards for unusual activity
- ✅ Set spending limits in provider dashboards (if available)

**Spending Limits:**
- OpenAI: Settings → Billing → Usage limits
- Anthropic: Settings → Billing → Set budget
- Google: Cloud Console → Billing → Budgets

---

## Keyboard Shortcuts

- **Enter** - Send message
- **Shift+Enter** - New line in message
- **Esc** - Close modal/panel (when focused)

---

## FAQs

### Is my data private?

**Yes!** Everything is stored locally in your browser:
- Chat history → Browser storage
- API keys → Encrypted in browser storage
- No cloud storage, no server tracking

### Can I use multiple providers at once?

**Yes!** Configure as many providers as you want. The system uses one at a time but automatically falls back if needed.

### What happens if I forget my password?

You'll need to clear your API keys and re-add them with a new password. Your chat history is NOT affected.

### How do I remove a provider?

1. Open AI Terminal
2. Click ⚙️ Settings
3. Find the provider
4. Click **Remove** button

### Can I export my chat history?

Yes! Use the .brain export feature in Settings. Your entire chat history is included.

### Which provider is best?

**For free:** OpenRouter (most versatile) or Groq (fastest)
**For paid:** Claude 3.5 Sonnet (best quality) or GPT-4o (most popular)

### Do I need to pay for AI providers?

No! 4 providers offer free tiers:
- OpenRouter
- Groq
- HuggingFace
- Mistral

### How much do paid providers cost?

Typically $0.15-$15 per 1 million tokens. For reference:
- 1M tokens ≈ 750,000 words
- Average conversation: 500-2000 tokens
- You can have hundreds/thousands of conversations for $1

---

## Getting Help

### Provider Issues

Each provider has their own support:
- **OpenRouter:** [openrouter.ai/docs](https://openrouter.ai/docs)
- **Groq:** [console.groq.com/docs](https://console.groq.com/docs)
- **HuggingFace:** [huggingface.co/docs](https://huggingface.co/docs)
- **Mistral:** [docs.mistral.ai](https://docs.mistral.ai)
- **OpenAI:** [platform.openai.com/docs](https://platform.openai.com/docs)
- **Anthropic:** [docs.anthropic.com](https://docs.anthropic.com)
- **Gemini:** [ai.google.dev/docs](https://ai.google.dev/docs)
- **xAI:** [docs.x.ai](https://docs.x.ai)

### Technical Issues

For bugs or feature requests, please file an issue on our GitHub repository.

---

## Changelog

### Version 2.0 (November 2025)
- ✅ Multi-provider support (8 providers)
- ✅ Encrypted API key storage
- ✅ Automatic fallback system
- ✅ Usage tracking per provider
- ✅ Model selector with filtering
- ✅ Free tier support

### Version 1.0 (Previous)
- Single provider (Google Gemini)
- Plaintext API key storage
- No fallback

---

**Enjoy your AI Terminal!** 🚀

If you have questions or feedback, we'd love to hear from you.

# Feature Map & Taxonomy

**Last Updated:** November 25, 2025
**Purpose:** Complete feature inventory and categorization
**Audience:** Product, engineering, design

---

## Feature Status Legend

✅ **Shipped** - Live in production
🚧 **In Progress** - Currently being developed
📋 **Planned** - Roadmapped with timeline
💡 **Idea** - Backlog, no timeline
❌ **Not Planned** - Out of scope

---

## 1. Notes & Knowledge Management

### 1.1 Core Notes (P0 - Q1 2026) ✅ SHIPPED November 2025
- ✅ Rich text editor (Lexical) - Shipped Nov 2025
- ✅ Markdown editing mode - Shipped Nov 2025
- ✅ Folder hierarchy (unlimited depth) - Shipped Nov 2025
- ✅ Note creation/deletion with undo - Shipped Nov 2025
- ✅ Full-text search with UI (Cmd+K) - Shipped Nov 2025
- ✅ Auto-save (debounced, IndexedDB) - Shipped Nov 2025

# Backup & Sync Guide

**Last Updated:** November 16, 2025
**Version:** 1.0

---

## Table of Contents

1. [Why Backup?](#why-backup)
2. [How Auto-Save Works](#how-auto-save-works)
3. [Setting Up Your Backup Folder](#setting-up-your-backup-folder)
4. [Cloud Provider Instructions](#cloud-provider-instructions)
   - [iCloud Drive (macOS)](#icloud-drive-macos)
   - [iCloud Drive (Windows)](#icloud-drive-windows)
   - [Google Drive (macOS)](#google-drive-macos)
   - [Google Drive (Windows)](#google-drive-windows)
   - [OneDrive (macOS)](#onedrive-macos)
   - [OneDrive (Windows)](#onedrive-windows)
   - [Proton Drive (macOS)](#proton-drive-macos)
   - [Proton Drive (Windows)](#proton-drive-windows)
   - [Dropbox (macOS)](#dropbox-macos)
   - [Dropbox (Windows)](#dropbox-windows)
5. [Syncing Across Devices](#syncing-across-devices)
6. [Restoring from Backup](#restoring-from-backup)
7. [Troubleshooting](#troubleshooting)
8. [FAQ](#faq)

---

## Why Backup?

### Your Data is Stored Locally

Neuman Brain stores all your data **locally in your browser** using IndexedDB. This ensures:

✅ **100% Privacy** - Your data never leaves your device
✅ **Zero Tracking** - No servers, no analytics, no third parties
✅ **Full Control** - You own your data completely

However, local storage also means:

⚠️ **Data can be lost if:**
- Your browser cache is cleared
- Your computer crashes or is lost/stolen
- You switch to a different browser or device
- Browser storage becomes corrupted

### Auto-Save Protects You

Auto-save automatically backs up your data to a **cloud-synced folder** on your computer:

✅ **Automatic backups** every 30 seconds
✅ **Sync across all your devices** via your cloud provider
✅ **Still 100% private** (you control the folder, not us)
✅ **Easy restore** if anything goes wrong

**Important:** Neuman Brain **never has access** to your backup files. They're stored in **your** cloud folder that **you** control.

---

## How Auto-Save Works

### The Technical Details

1. **You choose a folder** on your computer using the native folder picker
2. **Neuman Brain saves backups** to that folder every 30 seconds (configurable)
3. **Your cloud provider syncs** the folder to the cloud (iCloud, Google Drive, etc.)
4. **The cloud provider syncs** the folder to your other devices
5. **You can restore** from any backup file on any device

### What Gets Backed Up?

**Everything:**
- Notes (all content, formatting, images)
- Tasks (Kanban boards, todos, subtasks)
- Calendar events (all events, recurring events)
- Time tracking data
- Habits tracking
- All settings and preferences

### Backup File Format

Backups are saved as `.brain` files:

```
Neuman.Brain-2025-11-16-143022.brain
```

**Naming format:** `[CustomName]-YYYY-MM-DD-HHmmss.brain`

**File details:**
- Compressed with gzip (~70% size reduction)
- JSON format (human-readable when decompressed)
- Includes metadata (export date, version, item count)

### Versioning

Auto-save keeps **multiple versions** of your backups (default: 7):

```
.neuman-backups/
├── Neuman.Brain-2025-11-16-143022.brain  (most recent)
├── Neuman.Brain-2025-11-16-140511.brain
├── Neuman.Brain-2025-11-16-133045.brain
├── Neuman.Brain-2025-11-16-125512.brain
├── Neuman.Brain-2025-11-16-121834.brain
├── Neuman.Brain-2025-11-16-114256.brain
└── Neuman.Brain-2025-11-16-110723.brain
```

Older backups are automatically deleted when the limit is reached.

---

## Setting Up Your Backup Folder

### Step 1: Choose a Cloud Provider

Pick one of these cloud storage services (or use one you already have):

| Provider | Free Storage | Best For |
|----------|--------------|----------|
| **Google Drive** | 15GB | Most storage, works everywhere |
| **iCloud Drive** | 5GB | Apple users (seamless across Mac/iPhone/iPad) |
| **OneDrive** | 5GB | Windows users (built-in) |
| **Proton Drive** | 1GB (encrypted) | Privacy-focused users |
| **Dropbox** | 2GB | Reliable, widely supported |

**Don't have one?** [See detailed provider comparison](#choosing-a-cloud-provider)

### Step 2: Install the Desktop App

**Critical:** You must install your cloud provider's **desktop app** for automatic syncing.

- **iCloud Drive:** Built into macOS, or download for Windows
- **Google Drive:** Download from [google.com/drive/download](https://google.com/drive/download)
- **OneDrive:** Built into Windows 10/11, or download for macOS
- **Proton Drive:** Download from [proton.me/drive](https://proton.me/drive)
- **Dropbox:** Download from [dropbox.com/install](https://dropbox.com/install)

### Step 3: Create a Backup Folder

1. Open Finder (macOS) or File Explorer (Windows)
2. Navigate to your cloud-synced folder
3. Create a new folder named **"Neuman Brain Backups"** (or any name you prefer)

**Example paths:**
- **iCloud Drive:** `~/Library/Mobile Documents/com~apple~CloudDocs/Neuman Brain Backups`
- **Google Drive:** `~/Google Drive/Neuman Brain Backups`

# Phantom Shell User Guide

**Browser-Native Development Environment**

---

## What is Phantom Shell?

Phantom Shell is a **browser-native terminal** that runs directly in your browser. It provides a real development environment without installing anything on your computer.

**Key Features:**
- Full terminal emulator (not a chat interface)
- Runs Node.js and npm commands in your browser via WebContainers
- Command history with up/down arrow navigation
- Project persistence (saved to IndexedDB)
- Works entirely offline after initial load
- Zero data sent to servers - privacy-first

---

## Accessing Phantom Shell

1. Click the **AI Terminal** button (💬) in the header or press `Ctrl+Shift+A` / `Cmd+Shift+A`
2. You'll see two tabs at the top: **💬 Chat** and **⌨️ Shell**
3. Click **⌨️ Shell** to switch to Phantom Shell mode

---

## Built-in Commands

Phantom Shell supports several built-in commands (prefix with `/`):

### Help & Info

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `/help` | Show all available commands |
| `/version` | Show version info and WebContainer status |
| `/about` | About Phantom Shell |
| `/clear` | Clear the terminal screen |
| `/history` | Show your recent command history |

### Project Management

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `/projects` | List all your projects |
| `/new <name>` | Create a new project |
| `/open <name>` | Open an existing project |
| `/close` | Close the current project |
| `/export` | Export project to .brain file (coming soon) |

### AI Commands (Coming Soon)

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `/ai <prompt>` | Ask AI to help with development |

---

## Keyboard Shortcuts

| Shortcut | Action |
|----------|--------|
| `Enter` | Execute command |
| `↑` / `↓` | Navigate command history |
| `Ctrl+C` | Cancel current input |
| `Backspace` | Delete last character |

---

## System Commands (WebContainer)

Once WebContainer boots, you can run standard Node.js commands:

```bash
# Check Node.js version
node -v

# Initialize a project

# Neuman Brain - Privacy-First Personal & Work Management Platform

**Your second brain. Your data. Your device.**

Neuman Brain is a free, open-source platform combining **notes**, **tasks**, **projects**, and **time tracking** with a privacy-first, local-first architecture. Unlike cloud-only tools that lock in your data, Neuman Brain puts you in control.

[![MIT License](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-blue.svg)](LICENSE)
[![TypeScript](https://img.shields.io/badge/TypeScript-5.9-blue)](https://www.typescriptlang.org/)
[![React](https://img.shields.io/badge/React-19-blue)](https://react.dev/)
[![Tests](https://img.shields.io/badge/Tests-79%20passing-green)](https://vitest.dev/)

**Live Demo:** [brain.neuman.dev](https://brain.neuman.dev)

---

## 🔒 Privacy-First Architecture

**100% local-only. Zero cloud dependencies.** Your notes, tasks, and data stay on **your device** in your browser's IndexedDB (50GB+ capacity). No servers, no tracking, no data mining, no Google, no Firebase.

**Local backups only:** Export/import your data as .brain files (encrypted backups coming Q2 2026). Optional auto-save to local folder using File System Access API. The platform works fully offline, fully local, with zero external dependencies.

**Why this matters:**
- ✅ No vendor can access your private notes or tasks
- ✅ No monthly fees to access your own data
- ✅ Works offline forever (no internet required)
- ✅ Export your data anytime (JSON, Markdown)
- ✅ Zero tracking, zero analytics (unless you opt in)

---

## ✨ What Makes Neuman Brain Different?

| Feature | Neuman Brain | Notion | Obsidian | Monday.com | Apple Notes |
|---------|--------------|--------|----------|------------|-------------|
| **Cost** | 100% Free | $10-15/user/month | Free (Sync $10/mo) | $12-20/user/month | Free (Apple only) |
| **Data Privacy** | Local-first, you own | Cloud-only | Local files | Cloud-only | iCloud (Apple only) |
| **Platform** | Web, PWA (any device) | Web, apps | Desktop-first | Web, apps | Apple ecosystem only |
| **Offline** | Full offline support | Limited | Full offline | No | Requires iCloud |
| **Notes + Tasks** | Integrated ✅ | Separate | Plugin-based | Weak notes | No tasks |
| **Speed** | 60KB, <2s load | 2-4MB, slow | Fast | 3MB+, slow | Fast |
| **Open Source** | ✅ MIT License | ❌ | ⚠️ Source available | ❌ | ❌ |

---

## 🚀 Current Features (v2.0)

### 📝 Notes & Knowledge Management *(Coming Q1 2026)*
**Status:** 🔜 **Top Priority - In Development**

- Markdown editor with live preview
- Folders, tags, and powerful search
- [[Wikilinks]] and backlinks (PKM features)
- Rich media (images, files, attachments)
- Note-to-task linking
- Daily notes and templates
- Export to Markdown, PDF, HTML

**Why delayed?** We rebuilt the entire platform from scratch in React/TypeScript (v2.0) to establish a solid foundation. Notes are our #1 priority for Q1 2026.

### ✅ Current Task & Project Management

- **Kanban Board:** Drag-and-drop tasks across Todo → In Progress → Done
- **Calendar:** Monthly & weekly views, sync tasks with dates
- **Time Tracking:** Toggl-style timer, billable hours, project tracking
- **Advanced PM (planned):** Gantt charts, dependencies, resource management

### 🎮 Habit Tracking & Gamification *(Coming Q1 2026)*

- Daily habits with streak counters
- XP, levels, and achievement badges
- Visualizations (heatmaps, progress charts)
- Integrated with tasks (habits generate recurring tasks)

### 🛠️ IT & Helpdesk Features *(Planned)*

- Personal ticketing system (track issues, bugs, projects as tickets)
- Incident tracking (SEV0-4, timeline, postmortem)
- System monitoring (types defined, UI planned)

---

## 🏗️ Architecture & Tech Stack

**Frontend:**
- React 19 + TypeScript 5.9
- Vite 7 (lightning-fast builds)
- Zustand 5 (1KB state management)
- Tailwind CSS 3 (utility-first styling)

**Data Storage (Local-First):**
1. **Primary:** IndexedDB (50GB+ capacity, async, high-performance)
2. **Legacy:** LocalStorage (migrated to IndexedDB automatically)
3. **Backups:** Local .brain file export/import + optional auto-save to local folder

**Deployment:**
- Static hosting (Cloudflare Pages, Netlify, Vercel)
- Zero server costs for personal use
- Self-hostable (just serve the built `/dist` folder)

## 🚀 Quick Start

### For Users (No Installation Required)

Your data is stored locally in your browser. No account required.

### For Developers

- npm or pnpm

**Setup:**

```bash
# Clone repository
git clone https://github.com/travisjneuman/system.knowledgebase.git
cd system.knowledgebase

# Install dependencies
npm install

# Start development server
npm run dev
# Open http://localhost:5173

# Run tests
npm run test

# Build for production

```
